cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question est comprise comme portant de façon générale sur la mesure de la juste valeur selon IFRS 13, sans faits supplémentaires sur un instrument précis.\\\",\\n    \\\"Pour un actif, le « risque de défaut de la contrepartie » est compris comme un risque de crédit incorporé dans les hypothèses des participants de marché affectant le prix de l’actif.\\\",\\n    \\\"Pour un passif, en l’absence d’autres faits, la question est analysée au regard du risque de non-exécution du passif par l’entité elle-même, tel que visé par IFRS 13.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Oui pour un actif si le risque de défaut de la contrepartie est une caractéristique de risque que les participants de marché intégreraient dans le prix. Pour un passif, IFRS 13 impose surtout de refléter le risque de non-exécution, y compris le propre risque de crédit de l’entité, et non le défaut d’une contrepartie au sens large.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_of_an_asset\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur d’un actif\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation générale, IFRS 13 exige de mesurer la juste valeur d’un actif selon les hypothèses des participants de marché et en tenant compte des risques qu’ils utiliseraient dans le prix (IFRS 13 3, IFRS 13 22). Donc, si le risque de défaut de la contrepartie affecte le prix de sortie de l’actif dans le marché principal ou le plus avantageux, il doit être intégré à la juste valeur (IFRS 13 24).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’élément évalué doit être un actif mesuré à la juste valeur.\\\",\\n        \\\"Le risque de défaut de la contrepartie doit être pertinent pour le prix qu’un participant de marché paierait pour cet actif.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"La valorisation de l’actif doit intégrer ce risque de crédit s’il influence le prix de marché observé ou estimé.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"3\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When a price for an identical asset or liability is not observable, an entity measures fair value using another valuation technique that maximises the use of relevant observable inputs and minimises the use of unobservable inputs. Because fair value is a market‑based measurement, it is measured using the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability, **including assumptions about risk**. As a result, an entity’s intention to hold an asset or to settle or otherwise fulfil a liability is not relevant when measuring fair value.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"22\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall measure the fair value of an asset or a liability using **the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability**, assuming that market participants act in their economic best interest.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"24\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Fair value is **the price that would be received to sell an asset or paid to transfer a liability in an orderly transaction in the principal (or most advantageous) market at the measurement date under current market conditions (ie an exit price)** regardless of whether that price is directly observable or estimated using another valuation technique.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_of_a_liability\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur d’un passif\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"non\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, si la question vise le « défaut de la contrepartie » du passif, ce n’est pas le facteur explicitement requis par IFRS 13 pour la juste valeur du passif. IFRS 13 impose de refléter le risque de non‑performance du passif, y compris le propre risque de crédit de l’entité qui doit exécuter l’obligation, sur l’hypothèse d’un transfert du passif (IFRS 13 34, IFRS 13 42, IFRS 13 43).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Pour un passif, la juste valeur se focalise sur le risque de non-exécution de l’entité émettrice, pas sur un risque de défaut de contrepartie formulé de manière générale.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"34\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A fair value measurement assumes that a financial or non‑financial liability or an entity’s own equity instrument (eg equity interests issued as consideration in a business combination) is **transferred to a market participant at the measurement date**. The transfer of a liability or an entity’s own equity instrument assumes the following:\\\\n(a)A liability would remain outstanding and the market participant transferee would be required to fulfil the obligation. **The liability would not be settled with the counterparty or otherwise extinguished on the measurement date.**\\\\n(b)An entity’s own equity instrument would remain outstanding and the market participant transferee would take on the rights and responsibilities associated with the instrument. The instrument would not be cancelled or otherwise extinguished on the measurement date.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"42\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The fair value of a liability reflects the effect of **non‑performance risk**. Non‑performance risk includes, but may not be limited to, **an entity’s own credit risk** (as defined in IFRS 7 Financial Instruments: Disclosures). Non‑performance risk is assumed to be the same before and after the transfer of the liability.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"43\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When measuring the fair value of a liability, an entity shall take into account **the effect of its credit risk (credit standing)** and any other factors that might influence the likelihood that the obligation will or will not be fulfilled. That effect may differ depending on the liability, for example:\\\\n(a)whether the liability is an obligation to deliver cash (a financial liability) or an obligation to deliver goods or services (a non‑financial liability).\\\\n(b)the terms of credit enhancements related to the liability, if any.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778935260703}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"On suppose que l’élément concerné est bien mesuré à la juste valeur selon IFRS.\\\",\\n    \\\"On interprète « risque de défaut de la contrepartie » comme le risque de crédit du débiteur de l’actif, et non comme une notion générique de risque.\\\",\\n    \\\"Faute de faits supplémentaires, on distingue séparément le cas d’un actif, d’un passif isolé et d’une position de portefeuille relevant de l’exception de l’IFRS 13.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Oui pour un actif si le risque de défaut de la contrepartie est un facteur que les participants de marché intégreraient dans le prix. Non pour un passif isolé, où l’IFRS 13 vise le risque de non-exécution et le propre risque de crédit de l’entité. Oui aussi, sous conditions, pour une position nette de portefeuille visée par l’exception de l’IFRS 13.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_of_an_asset\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur d’un actif\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, le risque de défaut de la contrepartie doit être pris en compte pour un actif s’il influence le prix qu’un participant de marché paierait. L’IFRS 13 impose une mesure fondée sur les hypothèses des participants de marché et intégrant le risque, avec des données observables ou, à défaut, des données non observables ajustées (IFRS 13 3, IFRS 13 22, IFRS 13 87, IFRS 13 89).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"La juste valeur d’un actif financier doit refléter l’effet du risque de crédit/default du débiteur lorsque ce risque affecte le prix de sortie.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"3\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When a price for an identical asset or liability is not observable, an entity measures fair value using another valuation technique that **maximises the use of relevant observable inputs and minimises the use of unobservable inputs**. Because fair value is a market‑based measurement, it is measured using the **assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability, including assumptions about risk**. As a result, an entity’s intention to hold an asset or to settle or otherwise fulfil a liability is not relevant when measuring fair value.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"22\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall measure the fair value of an asset or a liability using the **assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability**, assuming that market participants act in their economic best interest.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"87\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Unobservable inputs shall be used to measure fair value to the extent that relevant observable inputs are not available, thereby allowing for situations in which there is little, if any, market activity for the asset or liability at the measurement date. However, the fair value measurement objective remains the same, ie an exit price at the measurement date from the perspective of a market participant that holds the asset or owes the liability. Therefore, unobservable inputs shall **reflect the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability, including assumptions about risk**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"89\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall develop unobservable inputs using the best information available in the circumstances, which might include the entity’s own data. In developing unobservable inputs, an entity may begin with its own data, but it shall adjust those data if reasonably available information indicates that other market participants would use different data or there is something particular to the entity that is not available to other market participants (eg an entity‑specific synergy). An entity need not undertake exhaustive efforts to obtain information about market participant assumptions. However, an entity shall **take into account all information about market participant assumptions that is reasonably available**. Unobservable inputs developed in the manner described above are considered market participant assumptions and meet the objective of a fair value measurement.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_of_a_liability\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur d’un passif\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"non\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Pour un passif isolé, la question posée sur le défaut de la contrepartie ne correspond pas au facteur requis par l’IFRS 13. La norme exige de refléter le risque de non-exécution du passif, y compris le propre risque de crédit de l’entité, et non le risque de défaut du créancier/contrepartie (IFRS 13 42, IFRS 13 43).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Pour un passif, la juste valeur se calibre sur le risque propre de l’émetteur/debiteur, pas sur le défaut de la contrepartie en tant que tel.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"42\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The fair value of a liability **reflects the effect of non‑performance risk**. Non‑performance risk includes, but may not be limited to, **an entity’s own credit risk** (as defined in IFRS 7 Financial Instruments: Disclosures). Non‑performance risk is assumed to be the same before and after the transfer of the liability.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"43\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When measuring the fair value of a liability, an entity shall **take into account the effect of its credit risk (credit standing)** and any other factors that might influence the likelihood that the obligation will or will not be fulfilled. That effect may differ depending on the liability, for example:\\\\n(a)whether the liability is an obligation to deliver cash (a financial liability) or an obligation to deliver goods or services (a non‑financial liability).\\\\n(b)the terms of credit enhancements related to the liability, if any.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_3\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_of_a_group_position\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur d’une position de groupe\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, le risque de défaut de la contrepartie est pris en compte si l’on mesure une position nette de portefeuille sous l’exception de l’IFRS 13. La norme vise alors explicitement l’exposition nette au risque de crédit de la contrepartie, en tenant compte des accords de compensation/collatéral et de leur force exécutoire (IFRS 13 56).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’entité applique l’exception de l’IFRS 13 pour un groupe d’actifs et de passifs financiers avec une contrepartie déterminée.\\\",\\n        \\\"Des accords de compensation ou de collatéral existent et les participants de marché en tiendraient compte.\\\",\\n        \\\"L’évaluation reflète l’attente des participants de marché quant au caractère juridiquement exécutoire de ces accords en cas de défaut.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"En portefeuille net, la juste valeur peut intégrer l’exposition nette au risque de crédit de la contrepartie plutôt qu’une mesure instrument par instrument.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"56\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When using the exception in paragraph 48 to measure the fair value of a group of financial assets and financial liabilities entered into with a particular counterparty, the entity shall **include the effect of the entity’s net exposure to the credit risk of that counterparty or the counterparty’s net exposure to the credit risk of the entity** in the fair value measurement when market participants would take into account any existing arrangements that mitigate credit risk exposure in the event of default (eg a master netting agreement with the counterparty or an agreement that requires the exchange of collateral on the basis of each party’s net exposure to the credit risk of the other party). The fair value measurement shall **reflect market participants’ expectations about the likelihood that such an arrangement would be legally enforceable in the event of default**.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778935287517}

cache: keyv:repeat:0:exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py:4606dc2dc0f5c27588a548eb91f4dfc4f2d83a6b2066151c169a0cb3228e57d0:Lorsqu’une entité évalue un actif ou un passif à la juste valeur, doit-elle intégrer le risque de défaut de la contrepartie dans cette évaluation ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_12-39-53_promptfoo-eval-family-q5"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question est générale et ne décrit pas un instrument précis; l’analyse distingue donc l’évaluation d’un actif, celle d’un passif et, séparément, le cas particulier d’un portefeuille géré sur une base d’exposition nette.\\\",\\n    \\\"Le terme « risque de défaut de la contrepartie » est compris, pour un actif, comme le risque de crédit de la contrepartie/de l’émetteur pertinent pour le prix de sortie de marché; pour un passif, IFRS 13 vise plutôt le risque de non-exécution, y compris le propre risque de crédit de l’entité.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Oui, dans la mesure où ce risque fait partie des hypothèses que des participants de marché utiliseraient pour fixer la juste valeur. Pour un actif, le risque de crédit de la contrepartie peut être intégré; pour un passif, IFRS 13 impose surtout d’intégrer le risque de non-exécution, y compris le propre risque de crédit de l’entité.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"exit_price_for_an_asset\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Prix de sortie pour un actif\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation générale, si l’élément évalué est un actif, la juste valeur doit refléter les hypothèses des participants de marché et les risques qu’ils intégreraient dans un prix de sortie (IFRS 13 2, IFRS 13 3, IFRS 13 22, IFRS 13 24). Ainsi, si le risque de défaut de la contrepartie influence le prix auquel le marché achèterait l’actif, il doit être incorporé, y compris via des données non observables ajustées pour le risque si nécessaire (IFRS 13 87, IFRS 13 88, IFRS 13 89).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Pour un actif, intégrer le risque de crédit de la contrepartie lorsqu’il affecte le prix de marché de sortie.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"2\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Fair value is a market‑based measurement, not an entity‑specific measurement. For some assets and liabilities, observable market transactions or market information might be available. For other assets and liabilities, observable market transactions and market information might not be available. However, the objective of a fair value measurement in both cases is the same—to estimate the price at which an orderly transaction to sell the asset or to transfer the liability would take place between market participants at the measurement date under current market conditions (ie an exit price at the measurement date from the perspective of a market participant that holds the asset or owes the liability).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"3\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When a price for an identical asset or liability is not observable, an entity measures fair value using another valuation technique that maximises the use of relevant observable inputs and minimises the use of unobservable inputs. Because fair value is a market‑based measurement, it is measured using the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability, **including assumptions about risk**. As a result, an entity’s intention to hold an asset or to settle or otherwise fulfil a liability is not relevant when measuring fair value.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"22\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall measure the fair value of an asset or a liability using the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability, assuming that market participants act in their economic best interest.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"24\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Fair value is the price that would be received to sell an asset or paid to transfer a liability in the principal (or most advantageous) market at the measurement date under current market conditions (ie an exit price) regardless of whether that price is directly observable or estimated using another valuation technique.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"87\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Unobservable inputs shall be used to measure fair value to the extent that relevant observable inputs are not available, thereby allowing for situations in which there is little, if any, market activity for the asset or liability at the measurement date. However, the fair value measurement objective remains the same, ie an exit price at the measurement date from the perspective of a market participant that holds the asset or owes the liability. Therefore, unobservable inputs shall reflect the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability, **including assumptions about risk**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"88\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"**Assumptions about risk include the risk inherent in a particular valuation technique used to measure fair value** (such as a pricing model) and the risk inherent in the inputs to the valuation technique. A measurement that does not include an adjustment for risk would not represent a fair value measurement if market participants would include one when pricing the asset or liability. For example, it might be necessary to include a risk adjustment when there is significant measurement uncertainty (eg when there has been a significant decrease in the volume or level of activity when compared with normal market activity for the asset or liability, or similar assets or liabilities, and the entity has determined that the transaction price or quoted price does not represent fair value, as described in paragraphs B37⁠–⁠B47).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"89\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall develop unobservable inputs using the best information available in the circumstances, which might include the entity’s own data. In developing unobservable inputs, an entity may begin with its own data, but it shall adjust those data if reasonably available information indicates that other market participants would use different data or there is something particular to the entity that is not available to other market participants (eg an entity‑specific synergy). An entity need not undertake exhaustive efforts to obtain information about market participant assumptions. However, the entity shall take into account all information about market participant assumptions that is reasonably available. Unobservable inputs developed in the manner described above are considered market participant assumptions and meet the objective of a fair value measurement.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"transfer_price_for_a_liability\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Prix de transfert pour un passif\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Si l’élément évalué est un passif, IFRS 13 requiert explicitement que la juste valeur reflète le risque de non-exécution, y compris le propre risque de crédit de l’entité, et non principalement le défaut de la contrepartie au sens d’un actif (IFRS 13 42, IFRS 13 43). La mesure reste un prix de transfert fondé sur les hypothèses de marché et, faute de prix observable, peut reposer sur une technique d’évaluation du point de vue d’un participant de marché qui doit le passif (IFRS 13 36, IFRS 13 40, IFRS 13 41).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Pour un passif, intégrer le risque de non-exécution, y compris le propre risque de crédit de l’entité.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"36\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In all cases, an entity shall maximise the use of relevant observable inputs and minimise the use of unobservable inputs to meet the objective of a fair value measurement, which is to estimate the price at which an orderly transaction to transfer the liability or equity instrument would take place between market participants at the measurement date under current market conditions.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"40\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When a quoted price for the transfer of an identical or a similar liability or entity’s own equity instrument is not available and the identical item is not held by another party as an asset, an entity shall measure the fair value of the liability or equity instrument using a valuation technique from the perspective of a market participant that owes the liability or has issued the claim on equity.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"41\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"For example, when applying a present value technique an entity might take into account either of the following :\\\\n(a)the future cash outflows that a market participant would expect to incur in fulfilling the obligation, including the compensation that a market participant would require for taking on the obligation (see paragraphs B31⁠–⁠B33).\\\\n(b)the amount that a market participant would receive to enter into or issue an identical liability or equity instrument, using the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the identical item (eg having the same credit characteristics) in the principal (or most advantageous) market for issuing a liability or an equity instrument with the same contractual terms.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"42\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The fair value of a liability **reflects the effect of non‑performance risk**. **Non‑performance risk includes, but may not be limited to, an entity’s own credit risk** (as defined in IFRS 7 Financial Instruments: Disclosures). Non‑performance risk is assumed to be the same before and after the transfer of the liability.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"43\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When measuring the fair value of a liability, an entity shall take into account the effect of **its credit risk (credit standing)** and any other factors that might influence the likelihood that the obligation will or will not be fulfilled. That effect may differ depending on the liability, for example:\\\\n(a)whether the liability is an obligation to deliver cash (a financial liability) or an obligation to deliver goods or services (a non‑financial liability).\\\\n(b)the terms of credit enhancements related to the liability, if any.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_3\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"net_exposure_to_credit_risk\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Exposition nette au risque de crédit\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette approche ne s’applique dans cette situation que si l’entité détient un groupe d’actifs et de passifs financiers géré sur la base d’une exposition nette au risque de crédit ou de marché (IFRS 13 48). Dans ce cas particulier, IFRS 13 permet d’intégrer l’exposition nette au risque de crédit d’une contrepartie, en tenant compte des accords de compensation ou de collatéral si les participants de marché les considéreraient comme juridiquement exécutoires (IFRS 13 56).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’entité détient un groupe d’actifs financiers et de passifs financiers.\\\",\\n        \\\"Le groupe est géré sur la base de l’exposition nette au risque de marché ou au risque de crédit.\\\",\\n        \\\"L’évaluation concerne une contrepartie particulière et des accords de compensation/collatéral que les participants de marché considéreraient dans la mesure de la juste valeur.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si l’exception de portefeuille s’applique, la juste valeur peut refléter l’exposition nette au risque de crédit de la contrepartie.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"48\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity that holds a group of financial assets and financial liabilities is exposed to market risks (as defined in IFRS 7) and to the credit risk (as defined in IFRS 7) of each of the counterparties. **If the entity manages that group of financial assets and financial liabilities on the basis of its net exposure to either market risks or credit risk, the entity is permitted to apply an exception to this IFRS for measuring fair value.** That exception permits an entity to measure the fair value of a group of financial assets and financial liabilities on the basis of the price that would be received to sell a net long position (ie an asset) for a particular risk exposure or paid to transfer a net short position (ie a liability) for a particular risk exposure in an orderly transaction between market participants at the measurement date under current market conditions. Accordingly, an entity shall measure the fair value of the group of financial assets and financial liabilities consistently with how market participants would price the net risk exposure at the measurement date.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"56\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When using the exception in paragraph 48 to measure the fair value of a group of financial assets and financial liabilities entered into with a particular counterparty, the entity shall **include the effect of the entity’s net exposure to the credit risk of that counterparty or the counterparty’s net exposure to the credit risk of the entity** in the fair value measurement when market participants would take into account any existing arrangements that mitigate credit risk exposure in the event of default (eg a master netting agreement with the counterparty or an agreement that requires the exchange of collateral on the basis of each party’s net exposure to the credit risk of the other party). The fair value measurement shall reflect market participants’ expectations about the likelihood that such an arrangement would be legally enforceable in the event of default.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778935301228}

cache: keyv:repeat:1:exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py:4606dc2dc0f5c27588a548eb91f4dfc4f2d83a6b2066151c169a0cb3228e57d0:Lorsqu’une entité évalue un actif ou un passif à la juste valeur, doit-elle intégrer le risque de défaut de la contrepartie dans cette évaluation ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_12-39-53_promptfoo-eval-family-q5"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question vise une évaluation à la juste valeur au sens d’IFRS 13.\\\",\\n    \\\"Le terme « risque de défaut de la contrepartie » est compris, pour un actif, comme le risque de crédit de la partie débitrice lorsque ce risque influence le prix de marché de l’actif, et pour un passif, comme le risque de non-exécution incluant le propre risque de crédit de l’entité.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Oui, la juste valeur doit refléter les hypothèses des intervenants de marché. Pour un passif, IFRS 13 impose expressément d’intégrer le risque de non-exécution, y compris le propre risque de crédit; pour un actif, le risque de défaut n’est intégré que s’il influence le prix de marché de l’actif.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_of_an_asset\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur d’un actif\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, si l’élément évalué est un actif, IFRS 13 exige une mesure fondée sur les hypothèses des intervenants de marché et sur un prix de sortie, non sur l’intention propre de l’entité (IFRS 13 2, IFRS 13 3, IFRS 13 24). Donc le risque de défaut de la contrepartie doit être intégré seulement s’il fait partie des facteurs que des intervenants de marché prendraient en compte pour fixer le prix de cet actif (IFRS 13 22).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’élément évalué est un actif.\\\",\\n        \\\"Le risque de défaut de la contrepartie affecte les flux de trésorerie attendus ou les données de prix utilisées par les intervenants de marché.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Pour un actif, intégrer le risque de défaut uniquement dans la mesure où il influence le prix de sortie de marché.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"2\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Fair value is a market‑based measurement, not an entity‑specific measurement. For some assets and liabilities, observable market transactions or market information might be available. For other assets and liabilities, observable market transactions and market information might not be available. However, the objective of a fair value measurement in both cases is the same—to estimate the price at which an orderly transaction to sell the asset or to transfer the liability would take place between market participants at the measurement date under current market conditions (ie an exit price at the measurement date from the perspective of a market participant that holds the asset or owes the liability).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"3\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When a price for an identical asset or liability is not observable, an entity measures fair value using another valuation technique that maximises the use of relevant observable inputs and minimises the use of unobservable inputs. Because fair value is a market‑based measurement, it is measured using the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability, including assumptions about risk. **As a result, an entity’s intention to hold an asset or to settle or otherwise fulfil a liability is not relevant when measuring fair value.**\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"22\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall measure the fair value of an asset or a liability using **the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability**, assuming that market participants act in their economic best interest.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"24\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Fair value is the price that would be received to sell an asset or paid to transfer a liability in **the principal (or most advantageous) market at the measurement date under current market conditions (ie an exit price)** regardless of whether that price is directly observable or estimated using another valuation technique.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_of_a_liability\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur d’un passif\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, si l’élément évalué est un passif, IFRS 13 requiert expressément que la juste valeur reflète le risque de non-exécution, lequel comprend le propre risque de crédit de l’entité (IFRS 13 42). IFRS 13 impose aussi de tenir compte de la qualité de crédit et de tout autre facteur influençant la probabilité d’exécution de l’obligation (IFRS 13 43), et IFRS 9 confirme cette logique pour les passifs financiers évalués à la juste valeur (IFRS 9 5.7.7, IFRS 9 B5.7.13).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’élément évalué est un passif.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Pour un passif, inclure le risque de non-exécution, notamment le propre risque de crédit de l’entité, dans la juste valeur.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"42\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The fair value of a liability reflects **the effect of non‑performance risk. Non‑performance risk includes, but may not be limited to, an entity’s own credit risk** (as defined in IFRS 7 Financial Instruments: Disclosures). Non‑performance risk is assumed to be the same before and after the transfer of the liability.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"43\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When measuring the fair value of a liability, an entity shall take into account **the effect of its credit risk (credit standing) and any other factors that might influence the likelihood that the obligation will or will not be fulfilled**. That effect may differ depending on the liability, for example:\\\\n(a)whether the liability is an obligation to deliver cash (a financial liability) or an obligation to deliver goods or services (a non‑financial liability).\\\\n(b)the terms of credit enhancements related to the liability, if any.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"5.7.7\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall present a gain or loss on a financial liability that is designated as at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with paragraph 4.2.2 or paragraph 4.3.5 as follows:\\\\n(a)The amount of change in the fair value of the financial liability that is attributable to changes in **the credit risk of that liability** shall be presented in other comprehensive income (see paragraphs B5.7.13⁠–⁠B5.7.20), and\\\\n(b)the remaining amount of change in the fair value of the liability shall be presented in profit or loss\\\\nunless the treatment of the effects of changes in the liability’s credit risk described in (a) would create or enlarge an accounting mismatch in profit or loss (in which case paragraph 5.7.8 applies). Paragraphs B5.7.5⁠–⁠B5.7.7 and B5.7.10⁠–⁠B5.7.12 provide guidance on determining whether an accounting mismatch would be created or enlarged.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B5.7.13\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"IFRS 7 defines credit risk as ‘the risk that one party to a financial instrument will cause a financial loss for the other party by failing to discharge an obligation’. The requirement in paragraph 5.7.7(a) relates to **the risk that the issuer will fail to perform on that particular liability**. It does not necessarily relate to the creditworthiness of the issuer. For example, if an entity issues a collateralised liability and a non‑collateralised liability that are otherwise identical, the credit risk of those two liabilities will be different, even though they are issued by the same entity. The credit risk on the collateralised liability will be less than the credit risk of the non‑collateralised liability. The credit risk for a collateralised liability may be close to zero.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778935325300}

cache: keyv:repeat:1:exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py:4606dc2dc0f5c27588a548eb91f4dfc4f2d83a6b2066151c169a0cb3228e57d0:La juste valeur d’un instrument est déterminée dans une perspective de marché. Dans ce cadre, le risque de non-exécution ou de défaut de la contrepartie doit-il être reflété pour un actif comme pour un passif ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_12-39-53_promptfoo-eval-family-q5"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question est posée de manière générale dans le cadre d'une mesure à la juste valeur au sens d'IFRS 13, sans fait spécifique excluant l'application de ce cadre.\\\",\\n    \\\"On suppose que l'instrument est évalué à la juste valeur à la date de mesure et que l'objectif est de refléter le prix de sortie entre intervenants de marché.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Oui. Dans une perspective de marché, la juste valeur doit refléter les hypothèses des intervenants de marché sur le risque pour l'instrument évalué. Pour un actif, cela inclut le risque affectant les flux encaissables; pour un passif, cela inclut le risque de non-exécution, y compris le risque de crédit propre.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_of_an_asset\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur d’un actif\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, l'approche s'applique si l'instrument considéré est un actif: IFRS 13 impose une mesure fondée sur le marché, intégrant les hypothèses des intervenants de marché sur le risque et les flux attendus, qu'ils soient observables ou estimés (IFRS 13 3, 22, 87). Cela conduit à refléter le risque de défaut de la contrepartie ou tout risque affectant la réalisation des flux de l'actif, car une mesure sans prime de risque ne représenterait pas fidèlement la juste valeur (IFRS 13 88, B16).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"La valeur de l’actif doit intégrer le risque de défaut/recouvrement dans les flux attendus ou dans le taux d’actualisation selon les hypothèses de marché.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"3\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When a price for an identical asset or liability is not observable, an entity measures fair value using another valuation technique that maximises the use of relevant observable inputs and minimises the use of unobservable inputs. Because fair value is a market‑based measurement, it is measured using **the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability, including assumptions about risk**. As a result, an entity’s intention to hold an asset or to settle or otherwise fulfil a liability is not relevant when measuring fair value.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"22\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall measure the fair value of an asset or a liability using **the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability**, assuming that market participants act in their economic best interest.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"87\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Unobservable inputs shall be used to measure fair value to the extent that relevant observable inputs are not available, thereby allowing for situations in which there is little, if any, market activity for the asset or liability at the measurement date. However, the fair value measurement objective remains the same, ie an exit price at the measurement date from the perspective of a market participant that holds the asset or owes the liability. Therefore, unobservable inputs shall reflect **the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability, including assumptions about risk**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"88\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Assumptions about risk include **the risk inherent in a particular valuation technique used to measure fair value** (such as a pricing model) and **the risk inherent in the inputs to the valuation technique**. **A measurement that does not include an adjustment for risk would not represent a fair value measurement if market participants would include one** when pricing the asset or liability. For example, it might be necessary to include a risk adjustment when there is significant measurement uncertainty (eg when there has been a significant decrease in the volume or level of activity when compared with normal market activity for the asset or liability, or similar assets or liabilities, and the entity has determined that the transaction price or quoted price does not represent fair value, as described in paragraphs B37⁠–⁠B47).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B16\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Market participants generally seek compensation (ie a risk premium) for bearing the uncertainty inherent in the cash flows of an asset or a liability. **A fair value measurement should include a risk premium** reflecting the amount that market participants would demand as compensation for the uncertainty inherent in the cash flows. Otherwise, the measurement would not faithfully represent fair value. In some cases determining the appropriate risk premium might be difficult. However, the degree of difficulty alone is not a sufficient reason to exclude a risk premium.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_of_a_liability\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur d’un passif\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, l'approche s'applique si l'instrument considéré est un passif: IFRS 13 mesure la juste valeur comme le prix pour transférer le passif dans une transaction ordonnée et impose de refléter les hypothèses de marché sur le risque (IFRS 13 9, 15, 22, 24, 87). Le risque de non-exécution doit donc être inclus, y compris via la compensation exigée par un intervenant de marché pour assumer l'obligation et son risque; IFRS 9 confirme en soutien que le risque de crédit d'un passif influence sa juste valeur (IFRS 13 B31-B33; IFRS 9 B5.7.13).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"La valeur du passif doit inclure le risque de non-exécution, soit dans les flux de sortie, soit dans le taux d’actualisation, sans double comptage.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"9\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"This IFRS defines fair value as **the price that would be received to sell an asset or paid to transfer a liability** in an orderly transaction between market participants at the measurement date.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"15\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A fair value measurement assumes that the asset or liability is exchanged in an orderly transaction between market participants to sell the asset or **transfer the liability** at the measurement date under current market conditions.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"22\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall measure the fair value of an asset or a liability using **the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability**, assuming that market participants act in their economic best interest.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"24\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Fair value is the price that would be received to sell an asset or **paid to transfer a liability** in an orderly transaction in the principal (or most advantageous) market at the measurement date under current market conditions (ie an exit price) regardless of whether that price is directly observable or estimated using another valuation technique.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"87\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Unobservable inputs shall be used to measure fair value to the extent that relevant observable inputs are not available, thereby allowing for situations in which there is little, if any, market activity for the asset or liability at the measurement date. However, the fair value measurement objective remains the same, ie an exit price at the measurement date from the perspective of a market participant that holds the asset or owes the liability. Therefore, unobservable inputs shall reflect **the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability, including assumptions about risk**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B31\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When using a present value technique to measure the fair value of a liability that is not held by another party as an asset (eg a decommissioning liability), an entity shall, among other things, estimate the future cash outflows that market participants would expect to incur in fulfilling the obligation. Those future cash outflows shall include market participants’ expectations about the costs of fulfilling the obligation and **the compensation that a market participant would require for taking on the obligation**. Such compensation includes the return that a market participant would require for the following:\\\\n(a)undertaking the activity (ie the value of fulfilling the obligation; eg by using resources that could be used for other activities); and\\\\n(b)**assuming the risk associated with the obligation** (ie a risk premium that reflects the risk that the actual cash outflows might differ from the expected cash outflows; see paragraph B33).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B32\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"For example, a non‑financial liability does not contain a contractual rate of return and there is no observable market yield for that liability. In some cases the components of the return that market participants would require will be indistinguishable from one another (eg when using the price a third party contractor would charge on a fixed fee basis). In other cases an entity needs to estimate those components separately (eg when using the price a third party contractor would charge on a cost plus basis because the contractor in that case would not bear the risk of future changes in costs).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B33\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity can include a risk premium in the fair value measurement of a liability or an entity’s own equity instrument that is not held by another party as an asset in one of the following ways:\\\\n(a)by adjusting the cash flows (ie as an increase in the amount of cash outflows); or\\\\n(b)by adjusting the rate used to discount the future cash flows to their present values (ie as a reduction in the discount rate).\\\\nAn entity shall ensure that it does not double‑count or omit adjustments for risk. For example, if the estimated cash flows are increased to take into account the compensation for assuming the risk associated with the obligation, the discount rate should not be adjusted to reflect that risk.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B5.7.13\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"IFRS 7 defines credit risk as ‘the risk that one party to a financial instrument will cause a financial loss for the other party by failing to discharge an obligation’. The requirement in paragraph 5.7.7(a) relates to **the risk that the issuer will fail to perform on that particular liability**. It does not necessarily relate to the creditworthiness of the issuer. For example, if an entity issues a collateralised liability and a non‑collateralised liability that are otherwise identical, the credit risk of those two liabilities will be different, even though they are issued by the same entity. The credit risk on the collateralised liability will be less than the credit risk of the non‑collateralised liability. The credit risk for a collateralised liability may be close to zero.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778935384024}

cache: keyv:repeat:0:exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py:4606dc2dc0f5c27588a548eb91f4dfc4f2d83a6b2066151c169a0cb3228e57d0:Comment le risque de crédit de la contrepartie intervient-il dans la mesure de la juste valeur d’un actif ou d’un passif selon les IFRS ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_12-39-53_promptfoo-eval-family-q5"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question est comprise comme une question générale de mesure de la juste valeur en IFRS, sans faits spécifiques sur le type d’instrument, le marché ou la technique d’évaluation.\\\",\\n    \\\"On suppose que la juste valeur est mesurée à la date d’évaluation selon IFRS 13, et que l’analyse doit couvrir séparément un actif isolé, un passif isolé et, le cas échéant, un portefeuille éligible géré sur une base nette.\\\",\\n    \\\"La référence au risque de crédit de la contrepartie est comprise, pour un actif, comme le risque de non-exécution du débiteur/contrepartie, et pour un passif, comme le risque de crédit propre de l’entité obligée.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Oui, le risque de crédit intervient dans la juste valeur, mais sa forme dépend de l’objet mesuré. Pour un actif, il entre via les hypothèses de marché et les ajustements de risque; pour un passif, via le risque de non-exécution incluant le risque de crédit propre; pour un portefeuille, seulement si l’exception de net exposure d’IFRS 13 est remplie.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"stand_alone_asset_fair_value\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur d’un actif pris isolément\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation générale, si l’élément mesuré est un actif, IFRS 13 impose une juste valeur fondée sur un prix de sortie et sur les hypothèses des participants au marché, non sur l’intention de l’entité (IFRS 13 2, 22, 24). Le risque de crédit de la contrepartie intervient alors s’il affecte les flux ou le prix qu’un participant au marché retiendrait, y compris via des ajustements de risque dans les techniques d’évaluation (IFRS 13 87, 88, B13).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Pour un actif, il faut incorporer dans les inputs ou ajustements de valorisation le risque de défaut du débiteur/contrepartie tel que le marché le prixerait.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"2\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Fair value is a market‑based measurement, not an entity‑specific measurement. For some assets and liabilities, observable market transactions or market information might be available. For other assets and liabilities, observable market transactions and market information might not be available. However, the objective of a fair value measurement in both cases is the same—**to estimate the price at which an orderly transaction to sell the asset or to transfer the liability would take place between market participants at the measurement date under current market conditions** (ie an exit price at the measurement date from the perspective of a market participant that holds the asset or owes the liability).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"22\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall measure the fair value of an asset or a liability **using the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability**, assuming that market participants act in their economic best interest.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"24\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Fair value is the price that would be received to sell an asset or paid to transfer a liability in an orderly transaction in the principal (or most advantageous) market at the measurement date under current market conditions (**ie an exit price**) regardless of whether that price is directly observable or estimated using another valuation technique.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"87\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Unobservable inputs shall be used to measure fair value to the extent that relevant observable inputs are not available, thereby allowing for situations in which there is little, if any, market activity for the asset or liability at the measurement date. However, the fair value measurement objective remains the same, ie **an exit price at the measurement date from the perspective of a market participant** that holds the asset or owes the liability. Therefore, unobservable inputs shall reflect **the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability, including assumptions about risk**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"88\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Assumptions about risk include the risk inherent in a particular valuation technique used to measure fair value (such as a pricing model) and the risk inherent in the inputs to the valuation technique. **A measurement that does not include an adjustment for risk would not represent a fair value measurement if market participants would include one** when pricing the asset or liability. For example, it might be necessary to include a risk adjustment when there is significant measurement uncertainty (eg when there has been a significant decrease in the volume or level of activity when compared with normal market activity for the asset or liability, or similar assets or liabilities, and the entity has determined that the transaction price or quoted price does not represent fair value, as described in paragraphs B37⁠–⁠B47).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B13\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Present value (ie an application of the income approach) is a tool used to link future amounts (eg cash flows or values) to a present amount using a discount rate. A fair value measurement of an asset or a liability using a present value technique captures all the following elements from the perspective of market participants at the measurement date: (a) an estimate of future cash flows for the asset or liability being measured. (b) expectations about possible variations in the amount and timing of the cash flows representing the uncertainty inherent in the cash flows. (c) the time value of money, represented by the rate on risk‑free monetary assets that have maturity dates or durations that coincide with the period covered by the cash flows and pose neither uncertainty in timing nor risk of default to the holder (ie a risk‑free interest rate). (d) **the price for bearing the uncertainty inherent in the cash flows (ie a risk premium).** (e) other factors that market participants would take into account in the circumstances. (f) for a liability, the non‑performance risk relating to that liability, including the entity’s (ie the obligor’s) own credit risk.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"stand_alone_liability_fair_value\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur d’un passif pris isolément\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Si l’élément mesuré est un passif, IFRS 13 exige explicitement que la juste valeur reflète le risque de non-exécution, lequel inclut le risque de crédit propre de l’entité (IFRS 13 42, 43). Pour un passif évalué par actualisation, les sorties de trésorerie et/ou le taux doivent aussi intégrer la compensation pour le risque que demanderait un participant au marché, sans double comptage (IFRS 13 B31, B33).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Pour un passif, la juste valeur doit intégrer le risque de crédit propre et, le cas échéant, les effets d’améliorations de crédit selon l’unité de compte IFRS 13.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"42\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The fair value of a liability **reflects the effect of non‑performance risk**. **Non‑performance risk includes, but may not be limited to, an entity’s own credit risk** (as defined in IFRS 7 Financial Instruments: Disclosures). Non‑performance risk is assumed to be the same before and after the transfer of the liability.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"43\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When measuring the fair value of a liability, an entity shall **take into account the effect of its credit risk (credit standing)** and any other factors that might influence the likelihood that the obligation will or will not be fulfilled. That effect may differ depending on the liability, for example: (a) whether the liability is an obligation to deliver cash (a financial liability) or an obligation to deliver goods or services (a non‑financial liability). (b) the terms of credit enhancements related to the liability, if any.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B31\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When using a present value technique to measure the fair value of a liability that is not held by another party as an asset (eg a decommissioning liability), an entity shall, among other things, estimate the future cash outflows that market participants would expect to incur in fulfilling the obligation. Those future cash outflows shall include **market participants’ expectations about the costs of fulfilling the obligation and the compensation that a market participant would require for taking on the obligation**. Such compensation includes the return that a market participant would require for the following: (a) undertaking the activity (ie the value of fulfilling the obligation; eg by using resources that could be used for other activities); and (b) **assuming the risk associated with the obligation** (ie a risk premium that reflects the risk that the actual cash outflows might differ from the expected cash outflows; see paragraph B33).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B33\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity can include a risk premium in the fair value measurement of a liability or an entity’s own equity instrument that is not held by another party as an asset in one of the following ways: (a) by adjusting the cash flows (ie as an increase in the amount of cash outflows); or (b) by adjusting the rate used to discount the future cash flows to their present values (ie as a reduction in the discount rate). **An entity shall ensure that it does not double‑count or omit adjustments for risk**. For example, if the estimated cash flows are increased to take into account the compensation for assuming the risk associated with the obligation, the discount rate should not be adjusted to reflect that risk.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B5.7.13\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"IFRS 7 defines credit risk as ‘the risk that one party to a financial instrument will cause a financial loss for the other party by failing to discharge an obligation’. The requirement in paragraph 5.7.7(a) relates to **the risk that the issuer will fail to perform on that particular liability**. It does not necessarily relate to the creditworthiness of the issuer. For example, if an entity issues a collateralised liability and a non‑collateralised liability that are otherwise identical, the credit risk of those two liabilities will be different, even though they are issued by the same entity. The credit risk on the collateralised liability will be less than the credit risk of the non‑collateralised liability. The credit risk for a collateralised liability may be close to zero.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_3\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"net_exposure_portfolio_fair_value\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur d’un portefeuille en exposition nette\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette approche ne s’applique dans la situation posée que si l’entité mesure un groupe d’actifs et de passifs financiers géré sur une base d’exposition nette, et non un instrument isolé (IFRS 13 48, 49). Dans ce cas seulement, la juste valeur peut inclure l’exposition nette au risque de crédit de la contrepartie ou au risque de crédit propre, en tenant compte des accords de compensation ou de collatéral juridiquement opposables (IFRS 13 56).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’élément mesuré est un groupe d’actifs et de passifs financiers, et non un actif ou passif isolé.\\\",\\n        \\\"Le groupe est géré sur la base de l’exposition nette à un risque de marché particulier ou au risque de crédit d’une contrepartie conformément à une stratégie documentée.\\\",\\n        \\\"Cette base nette est utilisée dans l’information fournie aux principaux dirigeants.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les instruments du groupe sont requis ou désignés à la juste valeur au bilan à chaque clôture.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si les critères sont remplis, l’évaluation tient compte du risque de crédit net de la contrepartie et des effets de netting/collatéral attendus par le marché.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"48\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity that holds a group of financial assets and financial liabilities is exposed to market risks (as defined in IFRS 7) and to the credit risk (as defined in IFRS 7) of each of the counterparties. If the entity manages that group of financial assets and financial liabilities on the basis of its net exposure to either market risks or credit risk, **the entity is permitted to apply an exception to this IFRS for measuring fair value**. That exception permits an entity to measure the fair value of a group of financial assets and financial liabilities on the basis of the price that would be received to sell a net long position (ie an asset) for a particular risk exposure or paid to transfer a net short position (ie a liability) for a particular risk exposure in an orderly transaction between market participants at the measurement date under current market conditions. Accordingly, an entity shall measure the fair value of the group of financial assets and financial liabilities consistently with how market participants would price the net risk exposure at the measurement date.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"49\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity is permitted to use the exception in paragraph 48 only if the entity does all the following: (a) **manages the group of financial assets and financial liabilities on the basis of the entity’s net exposure** to a particular market risk (or risks) or to the credit risk of a particular counterparty in accordance with the entity’s documented risk management or investment strategy; (b) provides information on that basis about the group of financial assets and financial liabilities to the entity’s key management personnel, as defined in IAS 24 Related Party Disclosures ; and (c) **is required or has elected to measure those financial assets and financial liabilities at fair value** in the statement of financial position at the end of each reporting period.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"56\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When using the exception in paragraph 48 to measure the fair value of a group of financial assets and financial liabilities entered into with a particular counterparty, the entity shall include **the effect of the entity’s net exposure to the credit risk of that counterparty or the counterparty’s net exposure to the credit risk of the entity** in the fair value measurement when market participants would take into account any existing arrangements that mitigate credit risk exposure in the event of default (eg a master netting agreement with the counterparty or an agreement that requires the exchange of collateral on the basis of each party’s net exposure to the credit risk of the other party). **The fair value measurement shall reflect market participants’ expectations about the likelihood that such an arrangement would be legally enforceable in the event of default.**\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778935434663}

Dans ce cadre, le risque de non-exécution ou de défaut de la contrepartie doit-il être reflété pour un actif comme pour un passif ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_12-39-53_promptfoo-eval-family-q5"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question est analysée au regard de la définition et des principes généraux de la juste valeur en IFRS, sans fait spécifique sur le type précis d’instrument ni sur une désignation particulière à la juste valeur par résultat.\\\",\\n    \\\"On suppose que l’instrument est mesuré à la juste valeur à la date de mesure considérée.\\\",\\n    \\\"On suppose que la question vise le contenu économique de la juste valeur dans une perspective de marché, et non la présentation du résultat ou des variations de juste valeur.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Oui. En IFRS 13, la juste valeur reflète les hypothèses des participants de marché, y compris le risque, pour un actif comme pour un passif. Pour un passif, cela inclut explicitement le risque de non-exécution; pour un actif, le risque de défaut de la contrepartie est reflété via les flux et/ou la prime de risque que les participants de marché intégreraient.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_of_an_asset\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur d’un actif\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, l’approche s’applique car IFRS 13 impose une mesure de juste valeur fondée sur des hypothèses de marché et non sur l’intention propre de l’entité (IFRS 13 2, 3, 22, 24). Pour un actif, si le risque de défaut de la contrepartie affecte les flux ou le prix que des participants de marché paieraient, il doit être reflété, y compris via une prime de risque; l’absence d’ajustement au risque ne représenterait pas fidèlement la juste valeur (IFRS 13 87, 88, B15, B16).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"La valorisation d’un actif doit intégrer le risque de défaut de la contrepartie dans les flux, le taux ou la prime de risque retenus par les participants de marché.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"2\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Fair value is a market‑based measurement, not an entity‑specific measurement. For some assets and liabilities, observable market transactions or market information might be available. For other assets and liabilities, observable market transactions and market information might not be available. However, the objective of a fair value measurement in both cases is the same—**to estimate the price at which an orderly transaction to sell the asset or to transfer the liability would take place between market participants at the measurement date under current market conditions** (ie an exit price at the measurement date from the perspective of a market participant that holds the asset or owes the liability).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"3\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When a price for an identical asset or liability is not observable, an entity measures fair value using another valuation technique that **maximises the use of relevant observable inputs and minimises the use of unobservable inputs**. Because fair value is a market‑based measurement, **it is measured using the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability, including assumptions about risk**. As a result, an entity’s intention to hold an asset or to settle or otherwise fulfil a liability is not relevant when measuring fair value.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"22\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall measure the fair value of an asset or a liability **using the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability**, assuming that market participants act in their economic best interest.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"24\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Fair value is the price that would be received to sell an asset or paid to transfer a liability in an orderly transaction in the principal (or most advantageous) market at the measurement date under current market conditions (ie an exit price) **regardless of whether that price is directly observable or estimated using another valuation technique**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"87\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Unobservable inputs shall be used to measure fair value to the extent that relevant observable inputs are not available, thereby allowing for situations in which there is little, if any, market activity for the asset or liability at the measurement date. However, **the fair value measurement objective remains the same, ie an exit price at the measurement date from the perspective of a market participant** that holds the asset or owes the liability. Therefore, **unobservable inputs shall reflect the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability, including assumptions about risk**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"88\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Assumptions about risk include the risk inherent in a particular valuation technique used to measure fair value (such as a pricing model) and the risk inherent in the inputs to the valuation technique. **A measurement that does not include an adjustment for risk would not represent a fair value measurement if market participants would include one when pricing the asset or liability.** For example, it might be necessary to include a risk adjustment when there is significant measurement uncertainty (eg when there has been a significant decrease in the volume or level of activity when compared with normal market activity for the asset or liability, or similar assets or liabilities, and the entity has determined that the transaction price or quoted price does not represent fair value, as described in paragraphs B37⁠–⁠B47).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B15\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A fair value measurement using present value techniques is made under conditions of
