uncertainty because the cash flows used are estimates rather than known amounts. **In many cases both the amount and timing of the cash flows are uncertain.** Even contractually fixed amounts, such as the payments on a loan, are uncertain if there is risk of default.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B16\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Market participants generally seek compensation (ie a risk premium) for bearing the uncertainty inherent in the cash flows of an asset or a liability. **A fair value measurement should include a risk premium reflecting the amount that market participants would demand as compensation for the uncertainty inherent in the cash flows.** Otherwise, the measurement would not faithfully represent fair value. In some cases determining the appropriate risk premium might be difficult. However, **the degree of difficulty alone is not a sufficient reason to exclude a risk premium**.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_of_a_liability\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur d’un passif\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, l’approche s’applique également car IFRS 13 mesure un passif selon le prix de transfert entre participants de marché à la date de mesure (IFRS 13 9, 15, 24). Pour un passif, les hypothèses de marché doivent inclure la compensation exigée pour assumer l’obligation et le risque que les sorties diffèrent des attentes; cela couvre le risque de non-exécution/non-performance dans la juste valeur (IFRS 13 22, 87, 88, B31, B33). IFRS 9 confirme en outre que, pour un passif financier à la juste valeur, le risque de crédit du passif est une composante distincte de la variation de juste valeur (IFRS 9 5.7.7, B5.7.13, B5.7.16).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"La valorisation d’un passif doit incorporer le risque de non-exécution, soit dans les flux attendus, soit dans le taux d’actualisation, sans double comptage.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"9\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"This IFRS defines fair value as **the price that would be received to sell an asset or paid to transfer a liability in an orderly transaction between market participants at the measurement date**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"15\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A fair value measurement assumes that the asset or liability is exchanged in an orderly transaction between market participants **to sell the asset or transfer the liability at the measurement date under current market conditions**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"24\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Fair value is the price that would be received to sell an asset or paid to transfer a liability in an orderly transaction in the principal (or most advantageous) market at the measurement date under current market conditions (ie an exit price) **regardless of whether that price is directly observable or estimated using another valuation technique**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"22\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall measure the fair value of an asset or a liability **using the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability**, assuming that market participants act in their economic best interest.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"87\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Unobservable inputs shall be used to measure fair value to the extent that relevant observable inputs are not available, thereby allowing for situations in which there is little, if any, market activity for the asset or liability at the measurement date. However, **the fair value measurement objective remains the same, ie an exit price at the measurement date from the perspective of a market participant** that holds the asset or owes the liability. Therefore, **unobservable inputs shall reflect the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability, including assumptions about risk**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"88\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Assumptions about risk include the risk inherent in a particular valuation technique used to measure fair value (such as a pricing model) and the risk inherent in the inputs to the valuation technique. **A measurement that does not include an adjustment for risk would not represent a fair value measurement if market participants would include one when pricing the asset or liability.** For example, it might be necessary to include a risk adjustment when there is significant measurement uncertainty (eg when there has been a significant decrease in the volume or level of activity when compared with normal market activity for the asset or liability, or similar assets or liabilities, and the entity has determined that the transaction price or quoted price does not represent fair value, as described in paragraphs B37⁠–⁠B47).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B31\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When using a present value technique to measure the fair value of a liability that is not held by another party as an asset (eg a decommissioning liability), an entity shall, among other things, estimate the future cash outflows that market participants would expect to incur in fulfilling the obligation. Those future cash outflows shall include **market participants’ expectations about the costs of fulfilling the obligation and the compensation that a market participant would require for taking on the obligation**. Such compensation includes the return that a market participant would require for the following:\\\\n(a) undertaking the activity (ie the value of fulfilling the obligation; eg by using resources that could be used for other activities); and\\\\n(b) **assuming the risk associated with the obligation (ie a risk premium that reflects the risk that the actual cash outflows might differ from the expected cash outflows**; see paragraph B33).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B33\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity can include a risk premium in the fair value measurement of a liability or an entity’s own equity instrument that is not held by another party as an asset in one of the following ways:\\\\n(a) by adjusting the cash flows (ie as an increase in the amount of cash outflows); or\\\\n(b) by adjusting the rate used to discount the future cash flows to their present values (ie as a reduction in the discount rate).\\\\n**An entity shall ensure that it does not double‑count or omit adjustments for risk.** For example, if the estimated cash flows are increased to take into account the compensation for assuming the risk associated with the obligation, the discount rate should not be adjusted to reflect that risk.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"5.7.7\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall present a gain or loss on a financial liability that is designated as at fair value through profit or loss in accordance with paragraph 4.2.2 or paragraph 4.3.5 as follows:\\\\n(a) **The amount of change in the fair value of the financial liability that is attributable to changes in the credit risk of that liability shall be presented in other comprehensive income** (see paragraphs B5.7.13⁠–⁠B5.7.20), and\\\\n(b) the remaining amount of change in the fair value of the liability shall be presented in profit or loss\\\\nunless the treatment of the effects of changes in the liability’s credit risk described in (a) would create or enlarge an accounting mismatch in profit or loss (in which case paragraph 5.7.8 applies). Paragraphs B5.7.5⁠–⁠B5.7.7 and B5.7.10⁠–⁠B5.7.12 provide guidance on determining whether an accounting mismatch would be created or enlarged.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B5.7.13\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"IFRS 7 defines credit risk as ‘the risk that one party to a financial instrument will cause a financial loss for the other party by failing to discharge an obligation’. **The requirement in paragraph 5.7.7(a) relates to the risk that the issuer will fail to perform on that particular liability.** It does not necessarily relate to the creditworthiness of the issuer. For example, if an entity issues a collateralised liability and a non‑collateralised liability that are otherwise identical, the credit risk of those two liabilities will be different, even though they are issued by the same entity. The credit risk on the collateralised liability will be less than the credit risk of the non‑collateralised liability. The credit risk for a collateralised liability may be close to zero.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs9\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B5.7.16\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"For the purposes of applying the requirement in paragraph 5.7.7(a), an entity shall determine the amount of change in the fair value of the financial liability that is attributable to changes in the credit risk of that liability either:\\\\n(a) as the amount of change in its fair value that is not attributable to changes in market conditions that give rise to market risk (see paragraphs B5.7.17 and B5.7.18); or\\\\n(b) **using an alternative method the entity believes more faithfully represents the amount of change in the liability’s fair value that is attributable to changes in its credit risk**.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778935531841}

cache: keyv:repeat:1:exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py:4606dc2dc0f5c27588a548eb91f4dfc4f2d83a6b2066151c169a0cb3228e57d0:Comment le risque de crédit de la contrepartie intervient-il dans la mesure de la juste valeur d’un actif ou d’un passif selon les IFRS ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_12-39-53_promptfoo-eval-family-q5"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question est comprise comme visant les principes généraux IFRS de mesure de la juste valeur, sans fait spécifique sur un instrument ou un portefeuille déterminé.\\\",\\n    \\\"On suppose qu’il s’agit d’une mesure de juste valeur entrant dans le champ d’IFRS 13.\\\",\\n    \\\"En l’absence d’éléments contraires, on distingue un actif ou passif individuel d’un éventuel portefeuille géré sur base nette.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Selon IFRS 13, le risque de crédit intervient dans la juste valeur via les hypothèses des participants de marché : pour un passif, il inclut le risque de non-exécution et le propre risque de crédit; pour un actif, les hypothèses de marché intègrent aussi le risque de crédit pertinent. Une mesure sur base nette du risque de crédit de contrepartie n’est admise que si les conditions précises de l’exception de portefeuille sont remplies.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"individual_fair_value_measurement\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Mesure individuelle de juste valeur\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation générale, le traitement de base est celui d’IFRS 13 pour un actif ou passif individuel. La juste valeur doit refléter les hypothèses des participants de marché, y compris les hypothèses de risque, et pour un passif elle inclut expressément le risque de non-exécution, dont le propre risque de crédit de l’entité (IFRS 13 22, 42, 43). Si des inputs observables manquent, l’entité utilise une technique de valorisation maximisant les données observables; les inputs non observables doivent encore refléter les hypothèses de marché sur le risque (IFRS 13 61, 87, B13).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"La juste valeur d’un instrument isolé doit intégrer le risque de crédit pertinent dans le prix de sortie, sans se limiter à l’intention propre de l’entité.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"22\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall measure the fair value of an asset or a liability using **the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability**, assuming that market participants act in their economic best interest.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"42\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The fair value of a liability **reflects the effect of non‑performance risk**. **Non‑performance risk includes, but may not be limited to, an entity’s own credit risk** (as defined in IFRS 7 Financial Instruments: Disclosures). Non‑performance risk is assumed to be the same before and after the transfer of the liability.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"43\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When measuring the fair value of a liability, an entity shall take into account **the effect of its credit risk (credit standing)** and any other factors that might influence the likelihood that the obligation will or will not be fulfilled. That effect may differ depending on the liability, for example:\\\\n(a)whether the liability is an obligation to deliver cash (a financial liability) or an obligation to deliver goods or services (a non‑financial liability).\\\\n(b)the terms of credit enhancements related to the liability, if any.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"61\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall use valuation techniques that are appropriate in the circumstances and for which sufficient data are available to measure fair value, **maximising the use of relevant observable inputs and minimising the use of unobservable inputs**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"87\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Unobservable inputs shall be used to measure fair value to the extent that relevant observable inputs are not available, thereby allowing for situations in which there is little, if any, market activity for the asset or liability at the measurement date. However, the fair value measurement objective remains the same, ie an exit price at the measurement date from the perspective of a market participant that holds the asset or owes the liability. Therefore, **unobservable inputs shall reflect the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability, including assumptions about risk**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B13\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Present value (ie an application of the income approach) is a tool used to link future amounts (eg cash flows or values) to a present amount using a discount rate. A fair value measurement of an asset or a liability using a present value technique captures all the following elements from the perspective of market participants at the measurement date:\\\\n(a)an estimate of future cash flows for the asset or liability being measured.\\\\n(b)expectations about possible variations in the amount and timing of the cash flows representing the uncertainty inherent in the cash flows.\\\\n(c)the time value of money, represented by the rate on risk‑free monetary assets that have maturity dates or durations that coincide with the period covered by the cash flows and pose neither uncertainty in timing nor risk of default to the holder (ie a risk‑free interest rate).\\\\n(d)**the price for bearing the uncertainty inherent in the cash flows (ie a risk premium).**\\\\n(e)other factors that market participants would take into account in the circumstances.\\\\n(f)**for a liability, the non‑performance risk relating to that liability, including the entity’s (ie the obligor’s) own credit risk.**\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"portfolio_exception\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Exception de portefeuille\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette approche ne s’applique dans cette situation que s’il existe un groupe d’actifs et de passifs financiers géré sur la base de l’exposition nette au risque de marché ou au risque de crédit d’une contrepartie (IFRS 13 48, 49). Si ces conditions sont remplies, la juste valeur peut inclure l’exposition nette au risque de crédit de la contrepartie, en tenant compte des accords de compensation ou collatéral que les participants de marché jugeraient juridiquement opposables (IFRS 13 56).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’entité gère un groupe d’actifs et de passifs financiers sur la base de l’exposition nette à un risque de marché ou au risque de crédit d’une contrepartie.\\\",\\n        \\\"Cette gestion sur base nette est conforme à une stratégie documentée et reportée aux principaux dirigeants.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les instruments concernés sont requis ou désignés à la juste valeur au bilan à chaque clôture.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si les conditions sont réunies, la juste valeur peut être mesurée sur une base nette de risque de crédit de contrepartie plutôt qu’instrument par instrument.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"48\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity that holds a group of financial assets and financial liabilities is exposed to market risks (as defined in IFRS 7) and to the credit risk (as defined in IFRS 7) of each of the counterparties. If the entity manages that group of financial assets and financial liabilities **on the basis of its net exposure to either market risks or credit risk**, the entity is permitted to apply an exception to this IFRS for measuring fair value. That exception permits an entity to measure the fair value of a group of financial assets and financial liabilities **on the basis of the price that would be received to sell a net long position (ie an asset) for a particular risk exposure or paid to transfer a net short position (ie a liability)** for a particular risk exposure in an orderly transaction between market participants at the measurement date under current market conditions. Accordingly, an entity shall measure the fair value of the group of financial assets and financial liabilities consistently with how market participants would price the net risk exposure at the measurement date.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"49\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity is permitted to use the exception in paragraph 48 only if the entity does all the following:\\\\n(a)**manages the group of financial assets and financial liabilities on the basis of the entity’s net exposure** to a particular market risk (or risks) or to the credit risk of a particular counterparty in accordance with the entity’s documented risk management or investment strategy;\\\\n(b)**provides information on that basis** about the group of financial assets and financial liabilities to the entity’s key management personnel, as defined in IAS 24 Related Party Disclosures ; and\\\\n(c)**is required or has elected to measure those financial assets and financial liabilities at fair value** in the statement of financial position at the end of each reporting period.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"56\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When using the exception in paragraph 48 to measure the fair value of a group of financial assets and financial liabilities entered into with a particular counterparty, the entity shall include **the effect of the entity’s net exposure to the credit risk of that counterparty or the counterparty’s net exposure to the credit risk of the entity** in the fair value measurement when market participants would take into account any existing arrangements that mitigate credit risk exposure in the event of default (eg a master netting agreement with the counterparty or an agreement that requires the exchange of collateral on the basis of each party’s net exposure to the credit risk of the other party). The fair value measurement shall reflect market participants’ expectations about **the likelihood that such an arrangement would be legally enforceable in the event of default**.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778935607107}

cache: keyv:repeat:0:exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py:4606dc2dc0f5c27588a548eb91f4dfc4f2d83a6b2066151c169a0cb3228e57d0:Pour mesurer la juste valeur, faut-il ajuster l’évaluation afin de tenir compte de la possibilité que la contrepartie ne remplisse pas ses obligations ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_12-39-53_promptfoo-eval-family-q5"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question vise une mesure de juste valeur relevant d’IFRS 13.\\\",\\n    \\\"Le terme « contrepartie » est compris comme le risque de crédit ou de non-exécution qu’un participant de marché intégrerait dans le prix à la date d’évaluation.\\\",\\n    \\\"Les faits ne précisent pas si l’élément évalué est un actif ou un passif; les deux approches identifiées doivent donc être examinées.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Oui, la juste valeur doit intégrer le risque de non-exécution lorsqu’un participant de marché le prendrait en compte. En pratique, pour un actif, cela vise surtout le risque de crédit de la contrepartie; pour un passif, IFRS 13 impose d’intégrer le risque de non-performance, y compris le risque de crédit propre.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_of_an_asset\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur d’un actif\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Si l’élément mesuré est un actif exposé au défaut de la contrepartie, l’évaluation doit refléter les hypothèses des participants de marché sur ce risque, car la juste valeur est une mesure de marché et inclut les hypothèses sur le risque (IFRS 13 3, IFRS 13 22). Lorsqu’il faut recourir à une technique d’évaluation, IFRS 13 exige de prendre en compte les hypothèses de marché et, si nécessaire, une prime de risque liée à l’incertitude des flux (IFRS 13 87, IFRS 13 88, IFRS 13 B16).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’élément évalué doit être un actif dont le prix de sortie serait affecté par le risque de non-exécution de la contrepartie.\\\",\\n        \\\"L’ajustement doit refléter les hypothèses de participants de marché à la date d’évaluation, et non une intention propre à l’entité.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"La valorisation de l’actif peut nécessiter un ajustement des flux ou du taux pour refléter le risque de crédit de la contrepartie tel que le marché le pricerait.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"3\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When a price for an identical asset or liability is not observable, an entity measures fair value using another valuation technique that maximises the use of relevant observable inputs and minimises the use of unobservable inputs. Because fair value is a market‑based measurement, it is measured using **the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability, including assumptions about risk**. As a result, an entity’s intention to hold an asset or to settle or otherwise fulfil a liability is not relevant when measuring fair value.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"22\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall measure the fair value of an asset or a liability using **the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability**, assuming that market participants act in their economic best interest.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"87\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Unobservable inputs shall be used to measure fair value to the extent that relevant observable inputs are not available, thereby allowing for situations in which there is little, if any, market activity for the asset or liability at the measurement date. However, the fair value measurement objective remains the same, ie an exit price at the measurement date from the perspective of a market participant that holds the asset or owes the liability. Therefore, **unobservable inputs shall reflect the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability, including assumptions about risk**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"88\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Assumptions about risk include the risk inherent in a particular valuation technique used to measure fair value (such as a pricing model) and the risk inherent in the inputs to the valuation technique. **A measurement that does not include an adjustment for risk would not represent a fair value measurement if market participants would include one when pricing the asset or liability.** For example, it might be necessary to include a risk adjustment when there is significant measurement uncertainty (eg when there has been a significant decrease in the volume or level of activity when compared with normal market activity for the asset or liability, or similar assets or liabilities, and the entity has determined that the transaction price or quoted price does not represent fair value, as described in paragraphs B37⁠–⁠B47).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B16\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Market participants generally seek compensation (ie a risk premium) for bearing the uncertainty inherent in the cash flows of an asset or a liability. **A fair value measurement should include a risk premium reflecting the amount that market participants would demand as compensation for the uncertainty inherent in the cash flows.** Otherwise, the measurement would not faithfully represent fair value. In some cases determining the appropriate risk premium might be difficult. However, the degree of difficulty alone is not a sufficient reason to exclude a risk premium.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"fair_value_of_a_liability\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Juste valeur d’un passif\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Si l’élément mesuré est un passif, IFRS 13 impose explicitement d’intégrer le risque de non-performance, y compris le risque de crédit propre de l’entité, dans la juste valeur (IFRS 13 42, IFRS 13 43). Ainsi, dans cette situation, l’ajustement ne porte pas sur la défaillance de la contrepartie au sens large, sauf cas particulier de mesure nette d’expositions où le risque de crédit de la contrepartie est aussi intégré (IFRS 13 56).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’élément évalué doit être un passif ou, le cas échéant, un groupe d’actifs et de passifs financiers mesuré sur une base d’exposition nette avec une contrepartie donnée.\\\",\\n        \\\"Pour un passif simple, l’ajustement vise le risque de non-performance propre, y compris le risque de crédit propre, et non le risque de crédit d’un garant séparé.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"La juste valeur du passif doit inclure le risque de non-performance propre; en cas d’exposition nette avec une contrepartie, le risque de crédit net de cette contrepartie peut aussi être intégré.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"42\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The fair value of a liability reflects **the effect of non‑performance risk**. Non‑performance risk includes, but may not be limited to, **an entity’s own credit risk** (as defined in IFRS 7 Financial Instruments: Disclosures). Non‑performance risk is assumed to be the same before and after the transfer of the liability.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"43\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When measuring the fair value of a liability, an entity shall take into account **the effect of its credit risk (credit standing) and any other factors that might influence the likelihood that the obligation will or will not be fulfilled**. That effect may differ depending on the liability, for example:\\\\n(a)whether the liability is an obligation to deliver cash (a financial liability) or an obligation to deliver goods or services (a non‑financial liability).\\\\n(b)the terms of credit enhancements related to the liability, if any.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"44\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The fair value of a liability reflects the effect of non‑performance risk on the basis of its unit of account. The issuer of a liability issued with an inseparable third‑party credit enhancement that is accounted for separately from the liability shall **not include the effect of the credit enhancement** (eg a third‑party guarantee of debt) in the fair value measurement of the liability. If the credit enhancement is accounted for separately from the liability, the issuer would take into account **its own credit standing and not that of the third party guarantor** when measuring the fair value of the liability.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"56\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When using the exception in paragraph 48 to measure the fair value of a group of financial assets and financial liabilities entered into with a particular counterparty, the entity shall include **the effect of the entity’s net exposure to the credit risk of that counterparty or the counterparty’s net exposure to the credit risk of the entity** in the fair value measurement when market participants would take into account any existing arrangements that mitigate credit risk exposure in the event of default (eg a master netting agreement with the counterparty or an agreement that requires the exchange of collateral on the basis of each party’s net exposure to the credit risk of the other party). The fair value measurement shall reflect market participants’ expectations about the likelihood that such an arrangement would be legally enforceable in the event of default.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778935682085}

cache: keyv:repeat:1:exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py:4606dc2dc0f5c27588a548eb91f4dfc4f2d83a6b2066151c169a0cb3228e57d0:Pour mesurer la juste valeur, faut-il ajuster l’évaluation afin de tenir compte de la possibilité que la contrepartie ne remplisse pas ses obligations ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_12-39-53_promptfoo-eval-family-q5"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question porte sur une mesure de juste valeur au sens général d’IFRS 13, sans précision sur un actif ou un passif particulier.\\\",\\n    \\\"On suppose qu’aucun prix observable pour un élément identique n’élimine la nécessité d’utiliser une technique d’évaluation incorporant des hypothèses de risque de marché.\\\",\\n    \\\"On suppose que le risque visé est le risque de non-exécution pertinent pour la juste valeur, y compris le risque de crédit lorsque celui-ci influence le prix qu’utiliseraient des participants de marché.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Oui. IFRS 13 exige que la juste valeur reflète les hypothèses de risque que retiendraient des participants de marché, y compris le risque de non-exécution et le risque de crédit lorsqu’ils influencent le prix (IFRS 13 22, 42, 43, 88). Cet ajustement peut être intégré soit dans les flux de trésorerie, soit dans le taux d’actualisation, sans double comptage (IFRS 13 B33).\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"risk_adjusted_cash_flows\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Flux de trésorerie ajustés du risque\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, IFRS 13 impose une juste valeur fondée sur les hypothèses de participants de marché et intégrant les risques pertinents, y compris le risque de non-exécution et l’incertitude de flux (IFRS 13 22, 42, 43, 87, 88). Si la possibilité que la contrepartie n’exécute pas ses obligations affecte les flux attendus, l’ajustement des flux est une méthode permise pour refléter ce risque dans la juste valeur (IFRS 13 B16, B33).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Ajuster les flux attendus pour refléter le risque de défaut ou de non-exécution que des participants de marché prendraient en compte.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"22\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall measure the fair value of an asset or a liability using **the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability**, assuming that market participants act in their economic best interest.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"42\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The fair value of a liability **reflects the effect of non‑performance risk**. Non‑performance risk includes, but may not be limited to, an entity’s own credit risk (as defined in IFRS 7 Financial Instruments: Disclosures). Non‑performance risk is assumed to be the same before and after the transfer of the liability.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"43\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When measuring the fair value of a liability, an entity shall **take into account the effect of its credit risk (credit standing) and any other factors that might influence the likelihood that the obligation will or will not be fulfilled**. That effect may differ depending on the liability, for example:\\\\n(a)whether the liability is an obligation to deliver cash (a financial liability) or an obligation to deliver goods or services (a non‑financial liability).\\\\n(b)the terms of credit enhancements related to the liability, if any.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"87\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Unobservable inputs shall be used to measure fair value to the extent that relevant observable inputs are not available, thereby allowing for situations in which there is little, if any, market activity for the asset or liability at the measurement date. However, the fair value measurement objective remains the same, ie **an exit price at the measurement date from the perspective of a market participant that holds the asset or owes the liability**. Therefore, unobservable inputs shall reflect **the assumptions that market participants would use when pricing the asset or liability, including assumptions about risk**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"88\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Assumptions about risk include the risk inherent in a particular valuation technique used to measure fair value (such as a pricing model) and the risk inherent in the inputs to the valuation technique. **A measurement that does not include an adjustment for risk would not represent a fair value measurement if market participants would include one when pricing the asset or liability**. For example, it might be necessary to include a risk adjustment when there is significant measurement uncertainty (eg when there has been a significant decrease in the volume or level of activity when compared with normal market activity for the asset or liability, or similar assets or liabilities, and the entity has determined that the transaction price or quoted price does not represent fair value, as described in paragraphs B37⁠–⁠B47).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B16\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Market participants generally seek compensation (ie a risk premium) for bearing the uncertainty inherent in the cash flows of an asset or a liability. **A fair value measurement should include a risk premium reflecting the amount that market participants would demand as compensation for the uncertainty inherent in the cash flows**. Otherwise, the measurement would not faithfully represent fair value. In some cases determining the appropriate risk premium might be difficult. However, the degree of difficulty alone is not a sufficient reason to exclude a risk premium.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B33\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity can include a risk premium in the fair value measurement of a liability or an entity’s own equity instrument that is not held by another party as an asset in one of the following ways:\\\\n(a)**by adjusting the cash flows (ie as an increase in the amount of cash outflows)**; or\\\\n(b)by adjusting the rate used to discount the future cash flows to their present values (ie as a reduction in the discount rate).\\\\nAn entity shall ensure that it does not **double‑count or omit adjustments for risk**. For example, if the estimated cash flows are increased to take into account the compensation for assuming the risk associated with the obligation, the discount rate should not be adjusted to reflect that risk.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"risk_adjusted_discount_rate\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Taux d’actualisation ajusté du risque\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, le même principe IFRS 13 conduit à incorporer le risque de non-exécution dans la valorisation si des participants de marché l’intègrent dans le prix (IFRS 13 2, 24, 61, 62, 88). IFRS 13 admet explicitement qu’un tel risque soit reflété par un ajustement du taux d’actualisation, à condition de ne pas compter deux fois le même risque si les flux sont déjà ajustés (IFRS 13 B17, B33).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Ne pas ajuster simultanément les flux et le taux pour le même risque de non-exécution.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Incorporer une prime de risque ou un ajustement de crédit dans le taux d’actualisation lorsque c’est ainsi que des participants de marché fixeraient le prix.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"2\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Fair value is a market‑based measurement, not an entity‑specific measurement. For some assets and liabilities, observable market transactions or market information might be available. For other assets and liabilities, observable market transactions and market information might not be available. However, **the objective of a fair value measurement in both cases is the same—to estimate the price at which an orderly transaction to sell the asset or to transfer the liability would take place between market participants at the measurement date under current market conditions** (ie an exit price at the measurement date from the perspective of a market participant that holds the asset or owes the liability).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"24\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Fair value is **the price that would be received to sell an asset or paid to transfer a liability in an orderly transaction in the principal (or most advantageous) market at the measurement date under current market conditions** (ie an exit price) regardless of whether that price is directly observable or estimated using another valuation technique.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"61\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall use valuation techniques that are appropriate in the circumstances and for which sufficient data are available to measure fair value, **maximising the use of relevant observable inputs and minimising the use of unobservable inputs**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"62\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The objective of using a valuation technique is to **estimate the price at which an orderly transaction to sell the asset or to transfer the liability would take place between market participants at the measurement date under current market conditions**. Three widely used valuation techniques are the market approach, the cost approach and the income approach. The main aspects of those approaches are summarised in paragraphs B5⁠–⁠B11. An entity shall use valuation techniques consistent with one or more of those approaches to measure fair value.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"88\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Assumptions about risk include **the risk inherent in a particular valuation technique used to measure fair value (such as a pricing model) and the risk inherent in the inputs to the valuation technique**. **A measurement that does not include an adjustment for risk would not represent a fair value measurement if market participants would include one when pricing the asset or liability**. For example, it might be necessary to include a risk adjustment when there is significant measurement uncertainty (eg when there has been a significant decrease in the volume or level of activity when compared with normal market activity for the asset or liability, or similar assets or liabilities, and the entity has determined that the transaction price or quoted price does not represent fair value, as described in paragraphs B37⁠–⁠B47).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B17\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Present value techniques differ in how they adjust for risk and in the type of cash flows they use. For example:\\\\n(a)The discount rate adjustment technique (see paragraphs B18⁠–⁠B22) uses **a risk‑adjusted discount rate** and contractual, promised or most likely cash flows.\\\\n(b)Method 1 of the expected present value technique (see paragraph B25) uses risk‑adjusted expected cash flows and a risk‑free rate.\\\\n(c)Method 2 of the expected present value technique (see paragraph B26) uses expected cash flows that are not risk‑adjusted and **a discount rate adjusted to include the risk premium** that market participants require. That rate is different from the rate used in the discount rate adjustment technique.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs13\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B33\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity can include a risk premium in the fair value measurement of a liability or an entity’s own equity instrument that is not held by another party as an asset in one of the following ways:\\\\n(a)by adjusting the cash flows (ie as an increase in the amount of cash outflows); or\\\\n(b)**by adjusting the rate used to discount the future cash flows to their present values** (ie as a reduction in the discount rate).\\\\nAn entity shall ensure that it does not **double‑count or omit adjustments for risk**. For example, if the estimated cash flows are increased to take into account the compensation for assuming the risk associated with the obligation, the discount rate should not be adjusted to reflect that risk.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778935763054}

cache: keyv:repeat:1:exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py:4606dc2dc0f5c27588a548eb91f4dfc4f2d83a6b2066151c169a0cb3228e57d0:Comment déterminer si un investisseur détient le pouvoir sur une entité ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_12-49-24_promptfoo-eval-family-q6"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question est générale et ne décrit aucun schéma de détention, aucun pacte contractuel, aucun droit potentiel ni aucune délégation de pouvoir.\\\",\\n    \\\"L’analyse est donc formulée comme une grille IFRS 10 de détermination du pouvoir, sans conclure qu’un investisseur donné contrôle effectivement une entité en l’absence de faits précis.\\\",\\n    \\\"Le contexte visé est celui d’un investisseur qui évalue le contrôle d’une entité au sens d’IFRS 10.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Oui. Le pouvoir se détermine selon IFRS 10 en identifiant les activités pertinentes, les droits existants substantifs et la capacité actuelle de les diriger. Selon les faits, ce pouvoir peut résulter de droits de vote, de droits contractuels, de droits potentiels substantifs, d’une qualité de principal, d’événements contingents ou d’un silo.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"majority_voting_rights\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Pouvoir par majorité des droits de vote\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette approche s’applique si, dans la situation analysée, les activités pertinentes sont dirigées par vote et que l’investisseur détient plus de la moitié des droits de vote substantifs. IFRS 10 précise alors qu’il a en principe le pouvoir, sauf si ses droits ne sont pas substantifs ou si une autre partie détient les droits actuels de diriger les activités pertinentes (ifrs10 B35, B36, B37).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Les activités pertinentes sont dirigées par les droits de vote.\\\",\\n        \\\"L’investisseur détient plus de 50 % des droits de vote substantifs.\\\",\\n        \\\"Aucune autre partie ne détient des droits actuels de direction des activités pertinentes.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si ces conditions sont réunies, l’investisseur conclut généralement qu’il a le pouvoir au sens d’IFRS 10.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B35\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An investor that holds more than half of the voting rights of an investee **has power** in the following situations, unless paragraph B36 or paragraph B37 applies: (a) **the relevant activities are directed by a vote of the holder of the majority of the voting rights**, or (b) a majority of the members of the governing body that directs the relevant activities are appointed by a vote of the holder of the majority of the voting rights.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B36\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"For an investor that holds more than half of the voting rights of an investee, to have power over an investee, the investor’s voting rights **must be substantive**, in accordance with paragraphs B22–B25, and **must provide the investor with the current ability to direct the relevant activities**... If another entity has existing rights that provide that entity with the right to direct the relevant activities and that entity is not an agent of the investor, **the investor does not have power over the investee**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B37\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An investor **does not have power** over an investee, even though the investor holds the majority of the voting rights in the investee, when those voting rights **are not substantive**.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"de_facto_power_through_voting_rights\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Pouvoir de fait par droits de vote\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette approche s’applique si l’investisseur détient moins de la majorité mais a, en pratique, la capacité unilatérale de diriger les activités pertinentes. IFRS 10 demande alors d’apprécier la taille relative de la participation, la dispersion des autres porteurs, les autres droits et les habitudes de vote pour déterminer si cette capacité pratique existe (ifrs10 B41, B42, B43, B45, B46).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’investisseur détient moins de 50 % des droits de vote.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les autres porteurs sont suffisamment dispersés ou passifs.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les faits montrent une capacité pratique de diriger unilatéralement les activités pertinentes.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"L’investisseur peut conclure au pouvoir même sans majorité formelle si les autres porteurs ne peuvent pas, en pratique, s’y opposer.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B41\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An investor with less than a majority of the voting rights **has rights that are sufficient to give it power when the investor has the practical ability to direct the relevant activities unilaterally**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B42\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When assessing whether an investor’s voting rights are sufficient to give it power, an investor considers **all facts and circumstances**, including: (a) the size of the investor’s holding of voting rights relative to the size and dispersion of holdings of the other vote holders... (b) potential voting rights... (c) rights arising from other contractual arrangements... and (d) any additional facts and circumstances... including **voting patterns at previous shareholders’ meetings**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B43\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When the direction of relevant activities is determined by majority vote and an investor holds **significantly more voting rights than any other vote holder** or organised group of vote holders, and the other shareholdings are **widely dispersed**, it may be clear... that the investor **has power** over the investee.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B45\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"However, the factors listed in paragraph B42(a)–(c) alone may not be conclusive. If an investor... is unclear whether it has power, it shall consider **additional facts and circumstances**, such as whether other shareholders are passive in nature as demonstrated by **voting patterns at previous shareholders’ meetings**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B46\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If it is not clear, having considered the factors listed in paragraph B42(a)–(d), that the investor **has power, the investor does not control the investee**.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_3\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"contractual_decision_making_rights\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Pouvoir par droits décisionnels contractuels\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette approche s’applique si le pouvoir ne découle pas principalement du vote mais de droits contractuels donnant la capacité actuelle de diriger les activités pertinentes. IFRS 10 vise expressément les droits de nomination, révocation, veto ou gestion contractuelle, en tenant compte de la conception de l’entité et de la capacité pratique de direction unilatérale (ifrs10 10, B15, B17, B18).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Les droits contractuels portent sur les activités qui affectent significativement les rendements.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les droits sont existants, substantifs et non seulement protecteurs.\\\",\\n        \\\"Ces droits donnent une capacité actuelle de diriger les activités pertinentes.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le pouvoir peut exister même sans majorité de vote si le contrat confère la direction actuelle des activités pertinentes.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"10\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An investor **has power over an investee when the investor has existing rights that give it the current ability to direct the relevant activities**, ie the activities that significantly affect the investee’s returns.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B15\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Examples of rights that, either individually or in combination, can give an investor power include but are not limited to: ... (b) **rights to appoint, reassign or remove members of an investee’s key management personnel** who have the ability to direct the relevant activities; (c) **rights to appoint or remove another entity that directs the relevant activities**; ... (e) other rights (such as **decision‑making rights specified in a management contract**) that give the holder the ability to direct the relevant activities.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B17\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When voting rights cannot have a significant effect on an investee’s returns... the investor needs to assess **those contractual arrangements** in order to determine whether it has rights sufficient to give it power over the investee.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B18\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In some circumstances it may be difficult to determine whether an investor’s rights are sufficient to give it power over an investee. In such cases... the investor shall consider evidence of whether it has the **practical ability to direct the relevant activities unilaterally**.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_4\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"substantive_potential_voting_rights\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Pouvoir par droits de vote potentiels substantifs\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette approche s’applique si l’investisseur détient des options, convertibles ou forwards qui sont substantifs et lui donnent la capacité actuelle de diriger les activités pertinentes. IFRS 10 impose de tenir compte des droits potentiels seulement s’ils sont substantifs, en appréciant leurs termes, leur finalité et leur combinaison avec d’autres droits (ifrs10 B47, B48, B49, B50).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Les droits potentiels sont substantifs.\\\",\\n        \\\"Ils sont exerçables de manière à conférer une capacité actuelle lorsque les décisions doivent être prises.\\\",\\n        \\\"Pris seuls ou avec d’autres droits, ils donnent le pouvoir de diriger les activités pertinentes.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Des droits non encore exercés peuvent suffire à établir le pouvoir s’ils sont substantifs au sens d’IFRS 10.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B47\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When assessing control, an investor considers its **potential voting rights** as well as potential voting rights held by other parties, to determine whether it has power... Those potential voting rights are considered **only if the rights are substantive**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B48\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When considering potential voting rights, an investor shall consider the **purpose and design of the instrument**, as well as the purpose and design of any other involvement the investor has with the investee.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B49\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the investor also has voting or other decision‑making rights relating to the investee’s activities, the investor assesses whether those rights, **in combination with potential voting rights**, give the investor power.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B50\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"**Substantive potential voting rights alone, or in combination with other rights, can give an investor the current ability to direct the relevant activities.**\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_5\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"principal_decision_maker\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Pouvoir via un décideur agissant en principal\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette approche s’applique si l’investisseur ou un gestionnaire dispose de droits décisionnels délégués. IFRS 10 exige alors de déterminer si le décideur agit en principal ou en agent, en appréciant l’étendue de son pouvoir, les droits des autres parties, sa rémunération et son exposition à la variabilité des rendements; un décideur révocable sans motif par une seule partie est agent (ifrs10 B58, B60, B61, B64, B65).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Il existe une délégation de droits décisionnels sur les activités pertinentes.\\\",\\n        \\\"Le décideur agit en principal et non en agent.\\\",\\n        \\\"Aucune partie unique ne détient un droit substantif de révocation sans motif du décideur.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le pouvoir est attribué au principal; un agent n’exerce pas le contrôle pour son propre compte.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B58\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When an investor with decision‑making rights (a decision maker) assesses whether it controls an investee, it shall determine whether it is a **principal or an agent**... **An agent... does not control the investee** when it exercises its decision‑making authority.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B60\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A decision maker shall consider the **overall relationship** between itself, the investee being managed and other parties involved with the investee... in determining whether it is an agent: (a) the scope of its decision‑making authority... (b) the rights held by other parties... (c) the remuneration... (d) the decision maker’s exposure to variability of returns...\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B61\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Determining whether a decision maker is an agent requires an evaluation of all the factors listed in paragraph B60 **unless a single party holds substantive rights to remove the decision maker** (removal rights) and can remove the decision maker **without cause**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B64\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"**Substantive rights held by other parties may affect the decision maker’s ability to direct the relevant activities** of an investee. Substantive removal or other rights may indicate that the decision maker is an agent.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B65\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When a single party holds substantive removal rights and can remove the decision maker **without cause, this, in isolation, is sufficient to conclude that the decision maker is an agent**.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_6\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"power_through_relevant_activities_upon_contingent_events\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Pouvoir sur activités pertinentes déclenchées par un événement\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette approche s’applique si les activités qui affectent significativement les rendements ne deviennent pertinentes qu’à la survenance d’un événement ou de circonstances données. IFRS 10 précise que la partie qui a le droit actuel de prendre ces décisions peut avoir le pouvoir avant même la survenance de l’événement, et que le caractère contingent du droit n’en fait pas, à lui seul, un droit protecteur (ifrs10 B53).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Les activités pertinentes n’existent qu’en cas d’événement ou de circonstances particulières.\\\",\\n        \\\"L’investisseur détient les droits de décision sur ces activités au moment pertinent.\\\",\\n        \\\"Ces droits ne sont pas seulement protecteurs.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le pouvoir peut être retenu dès l’origine si l’investisseur contrôle l’unique activité réellement décisive en cas d’événement déclencheur.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B53\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"For some investees, **relevant activities occur only when particular circumstances arise or events occur**... only the decisions about the investee’s activities when those circumstances or events occur can significantly affect its returns and thus be relevant activities. **The circumstances or events need not have occurred for an investor with the ability to make those decisions to have power.** The fact that the right to make decisions is contingent on circumstances arising or an event occurring **does not, in itself, make those rights protective**.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_7\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"control_of_a_deemed_separate_entity\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Pouvoir sur une entité réputée distincte (silo)\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette approche s’applique seulement si une portion de l’entité est juridiquement ou économiquement cantonnée de sorte que ses actifs déterminés sont l’unique source de paiement de passifs ou intérêts déterminés. IFRS 10 impose alors d’évaluer séparément le pouvoir, les rendements variables et le lien entre les deux sur cette portion; si elle est contrôlée, seule cette portion est consolidée (ifrs10 B76, B77, B78, B79).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Une portion de l’entité est ring-fenced au sens d’IFRS 10.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les actifs spécifiés ne servent qu’à régler des passifs ou intérêts spécifiés.\\\",\\n        \\\"L’investisseur détient le pouvoir sur les activités pertinentes de cette portion.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"L’analyse du pouvoir peut devoir être faite au niveau d’un silo plutôt qu’au niveau de l’entité entière.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B76\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An investor shall consider whether it treats a **portion of an investee as a deemed separate entity** and, if so, whether it controls the deemed separate entity.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B77\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An investor shall treat a portion of an investee as a deemed separate entity if and only if the following condition is satisfied: **Specified assets of the investee... are the only source of payment for specified liabilities of, or specified other interests in, the investee**... **all the assets, liabilities and equity of that deemed separate entity are ring‑fenced** from the overall investee.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B78\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When the condition in paragraph B77 is satisfied, an investor shall identify the activities that significantly affect the returns of the deemed separate entity and how those activities are directed in order to assess **whether it has power over that portion of the investee**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B79\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the investor **controls the deemed separate entity, the investor shall consolidate that portion of the investee**.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778935893391}

cache: keyv:repeat:0:exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py:4606dc2dc0f5c27588a548eb91f4dfc4f2d83a6b2066151c169a0cb3228e57d0:Comment déterminer si un investisseur détient le pouvoir sur une entité ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_12-49-24_promptfoo-eval-family-q6"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question est générale et ne décrit pas de faits précis sur l’entité, les droits détenus, ni les relations entre parties.\\\",\\n    \\\"L’analyse vise uniquement le critère de pouvoir au sens d’IFRS 10, dans le cadre de l’évaluation du contrôle d’une entité.\\\",\\n    \\\"En l’absence de faits précis, chaque approche est appréciée comme potentiellement applicable selon la structure réelle des droits et des relations en cause.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Selon IFRS 10, le pouvoir existe si l’investisseur détient des droits substantifs lui donnant la capacité actuelle de diriger les activités pertinentes. En pratique, il faut identifier les activités pertinentes, la source des droits (vote, contrat, délégation, relations de fait) et vérifier si ces droits sont substantifs et non protecteurs.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"voting_rights\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Droits de vote\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette approche s’applique si les activités pertinentes de l’entité sont dirigées par vote. IFRS 10 précise qu’un investisseur a le pouvoir lorsqu’il détient des droits existants donnant la capacité actuelle de diriger les activités pertinentes, et les droits de vote sont souvent le mécanisme central dans ce cas (IFRS 10 10, IFRS 10 B34-B35). Même sans majorité, le pouvoir peut exister si l’investisseur a la capacité pratique de diriger unilatéralement les activités pertinentes, après analyse de la dispersion des autres droits et des droits potentiels substantifs (IFRS 10 B41-B43, IFRS 10 B47-B50).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Les activités pertinentes doivent être dirigées par vote ou droits similaires.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les droits de vote ou droits potentiels doivent être substantifs et non purement protecteurs.\\\",\\n        \\\"Si l’investisseur n’a pas la majorité, il doit avoir la capacité pratique de diriger unilatéralement les activités pertinentes.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Il faut analyser le pourcentage détenu, la dispersion des autres actionnaires, les accords de vote et les droits potentiels substantifs.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"10\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An investor has power over an investee when the investor has **existing rights that give it the current ability to direct the relevant activities**, ie the activities that significantly affect the investee’s returns.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B34\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Often an investor has **the current ability, through voting or similar rights, to direct the relevant activities**. An investor considers the requirements in this section (paragraphs B35⁠–⁠B50) if the relevant activities of an investee are directed through voting rights.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B35\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An investor that holds **more than half of the voting rights of an investee has power** in the following situations, unless paragraph B36 or paragraph B37 applies:\\\\n(a)the relevant activities are directed by a vote of the holder of the majority of the voting rights, or\\\\n(b)a majority of the members of the governing body that directs the relevant activities are appointed by a vote of the holder of the majority of the voting rights.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B41\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An investor with less than a majority of the voting rights has rights that are **sufficient to give it power when the investor has the practical ability to direct the relevant activities unilaterally**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B42\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When assessing whether an investor’s voting rights are sufficient to give it power, an investor considers **all facts and circumstances**, including:\\\\n(a)the size of the investor’s holding of voting rights relative to the size and dispersion of holdings of the other vote holders, noting that:\\\\n(i)the more voting rights an investor holds, the more likely the investor is to have existing rights that give it the current ability to direct the relevant activities ;\\\\n(ii)the more voting rights an investor holds relative to other vote holders, the more likely the investor is to have existing rights that give it the current ability to direct the relevant activities;\\\\n(iii)the more parties that would need to act together to outvote the investor, the more likely the investor is to have existing rights that give it the current ability to direct the relevant activities;\\\\n(b)potential voting rights held by the investor, other vote holders or other parties (see paragraphs B47⁠–⁠B50);\\\\n(c)rights arising from other contractual arrangements (see paragraph B40); and\\\\n(d)any additional facts and circumstances that indicate the investor has, or does not have, the current ability to direct the relevant activities at the time that decisions need to be made, including voting patterns at previous shareholders’ meetings.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B43\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When the direction of relevant activities is determined by majority vote and an investor holds **significantly more voting rights than any other vote holder or organised group of vote holders**, and the other shareholdings are widely dispersed, it may be clear, after considering the factors listed in paragraph B42(a)⁠–⁠(c) alone, that the investor **has power over the investee**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B47\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"When assessing control, an investor considers **its potential voting rights as well as potential voting rights held by other parties**, to determine whether it has power. Potential voting rights are rights to obtain voting rights of an investee, such as those arising from convertible instruments or options, including forward contracts. Those potential voting rights are considered only if the rights are **substantive** (see paragraphs B22⁠–⁠B25).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B50\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"**Substantive potential voting rights** alone, or in combination with other rights, can give an investor the **current ability to direct the relevant activities**. For example, this is likely to be the case when an investor holds 40 per cent of the voting rights of an investee and, in accordance with paragraph B23, holds substantive rights arising from options to acquire a further 20 per cent of the voting rights.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"contractual_rights\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Droits contractuels\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette approche s’applique lorsque les droits de vote ne sont pas le facteur dominant et que les activités pertinentes sont dirigées par contrats ou autres droits décisionnels. IFRS 10 exige alors d’identifier l’objet et la conception de l’entité, les activités pertinentes et les droits existants qui donnent la capacité actuelle de les diriger (IFRS 10 B8, IFRS 10 B17, IFRS 10 B51-B53). Seuls les droits substantifs et non protecteurs sont pris en compte; un investisseur détenant seulement des droits protecteurs n’a pas le pouvoir (IFRS 10 B9, IFRS 10 B22-B28, IFRS 10 14).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Les droits de vote ne doivent pas être le facteur dominant de direction des activités pertinentes.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les droits contractuels doivent donner la capacité actuelle de diriger les activités pertinentes.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les droits doivent être substantifs et ne pas être seulement protecteurs.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Il faut examiner les contrats de gestion, droits de nomination/révocation, vetos décisionnels et clauses activées à certains événements.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"14\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An investor can have power over an investee even if other entities have existing rights that give them the current ability to participate in the direction of the relevant activities, for example when another entity has significant influence. However, **an investor that holds only protective rights does not have power over an investee**, and consequently does not control the investee.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs10\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B8\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An investee may be designed so that **voting rights are not the dominant factor in deciding who controls the investee**, such as when any voting rights relate to administrative tasks only and the relevant activities are directed by means of contractual arrangements. In such cases, an investor’s consideration of the purpose and design of the investee shall also include consideration of the risks to which the investee was designed to be exposed, the risks it was designed to pass on to the parties involved with the investee and whether the investor is exposed to some or all of those risks.
