Quels critères IFRS permettent de choisir entre une reconnaissance ponctuelle et une reconnaissance étalée ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_12-57-25_promptfoo-eval-family-q9"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question vise une licence de propriété intellectuelle entrant dans le champ d’IFRS 15.\\\",\\n    \\\"On suppose d’abord que la licence est une promesse distincte ; sinon, elle est combinée avec les autres biens ou services promis avant d’analyser le rythme de reconnaissance du revenu.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Selon IFRS 15, le choix entre reconnaissance ponctuelle et étalée dépend de la nature du droit transféré par la licence. Il faut d’abord vérifier si la licence est distincte, puis déterminer si elle donne un droit d’accès continu ou un droit d’utilisation figé à la date d’octroi.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"right_to_access_the_entitys_intellectual_property\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Droit d’accès à la propriété intellectuelle\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette approche s’applique si, dans la situation analysée, la licence est distincte et la promesse consiste à donner accès à la propriété intellectuelle tout au long de la période de licence (IFRS 15 B56, IFRS 15 B58). C’est le cas seulement si le contrat impose, ou le client s’attend raisonnablement à, des activités de l’entité qui affectent significativement cette propriété intellectuelle, que la licence expose directement le client à ces effets, et que ces activités ne transfèrent pas un bien ou service distinct (IFRS 15 B58, IFRS 15 B59A). Dans ce cas, le revenu est reconnu sur la durée, car le client reçoit et consomme simultanément les bénéfices de l’accès (IFRS 15 B60, IFRS 15 35(a)).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"La licence est distincte des autres biens ou services promis.\\\",\\n        \\\"Le client s’attend raisonnablement à des activités de l’entité affectant significativement la propriété intellectuelle pendant la période de licence.\\\",\\n        \\\"Ces activités n’aboutissent pas elles-mêmes au transfert d’un bien ou service distinct.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le revenu de licence est comptabilisé au fur et à mesure sur la période de licence.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B56\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the promise to grant the licence is distinct from the other promised goods or services in the contract and, therefore, the promise to grant the licence is a separate performance obligation, an entity shall determine whether the licence transfers to a customer either at a point in time or over time. In making this determination, an entity shall consider whether the nature of the entity’s promise in granting the licence to a customer is to provide the customer with either: **(a)a right to access the entity’s intellectual property as it exists throughout the licence period;** or (b)a right to use the entity’s intellectual property as it exists at the point in time at which the licence is granted.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B58\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The nature of an entity’s promise in granting a licence is a promise to provide a right to access the entity’s intellectual property if **all of the following criteria are met:** **(a)the contract requires, or the customer reasonably expects, that the entity will undertake activities that significantly affect the intellectual property** to which the customer has rights (see paragraphs B59 and B59A); **(b)the rights granted by the licence directly expose the customer to any positive or negative effects** of the entity’s activities identified in paragraph B58(a) ; and **(c)those activities do not result in the transfer of a good or a service to the customer** as those activities occur (see paragraph 25).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B59A\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity’s activities significantly affect the intellectual property to which the customer has rights when either: **(a)those activities are expected to significantly change the form** (for example, the design or content) **or the functionality** (for example, the ability to perform a function or task) of the intellectual property; or **(b)the ability of the customer to obtain benefit from the intellectual property is substantially derived from, or dependent upon, those activities.** For example, the benefit from a brand is often derived from, or dependent upon, the entity’s ongoing activities that support or maintain the value of the intellectual property.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B60\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the criteria in paragraph B58 are met, an entity shall account for the promise to grant a licence as a performance obligation **satisfied over time** because the customer will simultaneously receive and consume the benefit from the entity’s performance of providing access to its intellectual property as the performance occurs (see paragraph 35(a)).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"35\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity transfers control of a good or service over time and, therefore, satisfies a performance obligation and recognises revenue over time, if one of the following criteria is met: **(a)the customer simultaneously receives and consumes the benefits provided by the entity’s performance as the entity performs** (see paragraphs B3⁠–⁠B4);\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"right_to_use_the_entitys_intellectual_property\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Droit d’utilisation de la propriété intellectuelle\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette approche s’applique dans la situation analysée si la licence est distincte mais que les critères du droit d’accès ne sont pas remplis ; la promesse est alors de fournir un droit d’utiliser la propriété intellectuelle telle qu’elle existe à la date d’octroi (IFRS 15 B56, IFRS 15 B61). Le revenu est alors reconnu à un moment précis, lorsque le contrôle de la licence est transféré au client selon les indicateurs généraux de contrôle (IFRS 15 B61, IFRS 15 38). Les restrictions de temps, de territoire ou d’usage, ainsi que la défense d’un brevet, ne changent pas cette qualification (IFRS 15 B62).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"La licence est distincte des autres biens ou services promis.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les critères du paragraphe IFRS 15 B58 pour un droit d’accès ne sont pas remplis.\\\",\\n        \\\"La période de licence a commencé et le client peut effectivement utiliser et tirer parti de la licence.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le revenu de licence est comptabilisé en une fois à la date de transfert du contrôle, sans anticipation avant la mise à disposition effective.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B56\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the promise to grant the licence is distinct from the other promised goods or services in the contract and, therefore, the promise to grant the licence is a separate performance obligation, an entity shall determine whether the licence transfers to a customer either at a point in time or over time. In making this determination, an entity shall consider whether the nature of the entity’s promise in granting the licence to a customer is to provide the customer with either: (a)a right to access the entity’s intellectual property as it exists throughout the licence period; or **(b)a right to use the entity’s intellectual property as it exists at the point in time at which the licence is granted.**\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B61\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the criteria in paragraph B58 are not met, the nature of an entity’s promise is to provide **a right to use the entity’s intellectual property as that intellectual property exists (in terms of form and functionality) at the point in time at which the licence is granted to the customer.** This means that the customer can direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the licence at the point in time at which the licence transfers. **An entity shall account for the promise to provide a right to use the entity’s intellectual property as a performance obligation satisfied at a point in time.** An entity shall apply paragraph 38 to determine the point in time at which the licence transfers to the customer. However, **revenue cannot be recognised for a licence that provides a right to use the entity’s intellectual property before the beginning of the period during which the customer is able to use and benefit from the licence.**\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"38\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If a performance obligation is not satisfied over time in accordance with paragraphs 35⁠–⁠37, **an entity satisfies the performance obligation at a point in time.** To determine the point in time at which a customer obtains control of a promised asset and the entity satisfies a performance obligation, the entity shall consider the requirements for control in paragraphs 31⁠–⁠34. In addition, an entity shall consider indicators of the transfer of control, which include, but are not limited to, the following:\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B62\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall disregard the following factors when determining whether a licence provides a right to access the entity’s intellectual property or a right to use the entity’s intellectual property: **(a)Restrictions of time, geographical region or use**—those restrictions define the attributes of the promised licence, rather than define whether the entity satisfies its performance obligation at a point in time or over time. **(b)Guarantees provided by the entity that it has a valid patent to intellectual property and that it will defend that patent from unauthorised use**—a promise to defend a patent right is not a performance obligation because the act of defending a patent protects the value of the entity’s intellectual property assets and provides assurance to the customer that the licence transferred meets the specifications of the licence promised in the contract.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778936488080}

cache: keyv:repeat:0:exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py:4606dc2dc0f5c27588a548eb91f4dfc4f2d83a6b2066151c169a0cb3228e57d0:Dans le cadre d’une licence de propriété intellectuelle, faut-il comptabiliser le revenu lorsque le droit est mis à disposition ou l’étaler pendant toute la période d’utilisation autorisée ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_12-57-25_promptfoo-eval-family-q9"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"On suppose que la licence de propriété intellectuelle constitue une obligation de prestation distincte au sens d’IFRS 15, de sorte que les paragraphes spécifiques aux licences s’appliquent directement.\\\",\\n    \\\"On suppose que la question porte uniquement sur le moment de comptabilisation du revenu lié à la licence, hors autres biens ou services promis dans le contrat.\\\",\\n    \\\"En l’absence de faits complémentaires sur la nature de la propriété intellectuelle et sur les activités continues du concédant, il faut répondre de manière conditionnelle entre les deux modèles prévus par IFRS 15.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Sous IFRS 15, le revenu n’est pas automatiquement reconnu soit à la mise à disposition soit étalé dans tous les cas. Il dépend de la nature de la promesse: droit d’accès sur la durée si les critères d’IFRS 15 B58 sont remplis, sinon droit d’utilisation comptabilisé à un moment donné selon IFRS 15 B61.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"right_to_access_intellectual_property\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Droit d’accès à la propriété intellectuelle\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, l’étalement du revenu pendant la période autorisée s’applique seulement si la licence donne un droit d’accès et non un simple droit d’utilisation. IFRS 15 B58 exige que l’entité mène des activités affectant significativement la PI, que le client en subisse directement les effets, et que ces activités ne constituent pas un bien ou service distinct; dans ce cas, la prestation est satisfaite au fil du temps selon IFRS 15 B60 et IFRS 15 35(a).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Le contrat impose, ou le client s’attend raisonnablement à, des activités du concédant affectant significativement la propriété intellectuelle.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les droits concédés exposent directement le client aux effets positifs ou négatifs de ces activités.\\\",\\n        \\\"Ces activités ne donnent pas lieu au transfert d’un bien ou service distinct au fur et à mesure.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le revenu est comptabilisé sur la durée de la licence, selon une méthode de mesure de l’avancement conforme à IFRS 15 39–45.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B58\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The nature of an entity’s promise in granting a licence is a promise to provide a right to access the entity’s intellectual property if **all of the following criteria are met**: **(a) the contract requires, or the customer reasonably expects, that the entity will undertake activities that significantly affect the intellectual property** to which the customer has rights (see paragraphs B59 and B59A); **(b) the rights granted by the licence directly expose the customer to any positive or negative effects of the entity’s activities** identified in paragraph B58(a) ; and **(c) those activities do not result in the transfer of a good or a service to the customer as those activities occur** (see paragraph 25).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B60\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the criteria in paragraph B58 are met, an entity shall account for the promise to grant a licence as a **performance obligation satisfied over time** because the customer will simultaneously receive and consume the benefit from the entity’s performance of providing access to its intellectual property as the performance occurs (see paragraph **35(a)**). An entity shall apply paragraphs **39–45** to select an appropriate method to measure its progress towards complete satisfaction of that performance obligation to provide access.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"35\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity transfers control of a good or service over time and, therefore, satisfies a performance obligation and recognises revenue over time, if one of the following criteria is met: **(a) the customer simultaneously receives and consumes the benefits provided by the entity’s performance as the entity performs** (see paragraphs B3–B4);\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"right_to_use_intellectual_property\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Droit d’utilisation de la propriété intellectuelle\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, la comptabilisation à la mise à disposition s’applique si les critères du droit d’accès ne sont pas remplis. IFRS 15 B61 prévoit alors que la promesse est un droit d’utiliser la PI telle qu’elle existe au moment de l’octroi et que le revenu est reconnu à un moment donné; toutefois, il ne peut pas être reconnu avant le début de la période pendant laquelle le client peut utiliser et tirer bénéfice de la licence (IFRS 15 B61, IFRS 15 38).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Les critères d’IFRS 15 B58 relatifs au droit d’accès ne sont pas remplis.\\\",\\n        \\\"La licence a été transférée au client selon les indicateurs de contrôle applicables.\\\",\\n        \\\"La période pendant laquelle le client peut utiliser et tirer bénéfice de la licence a commencé.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le revenu est comptabilisé à un moment donné, lorsque la licence est transférée et devient utilisable par le client.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B61\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the criteria in paragraph B58 are not met, the nature of an entity’s promise is to provide a **right to use** the entity’s intellectual property as that intellectual property exists (in terms of form and functionality) **at the point in time at which the licence is granted to the customer**. This means that the customer can direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the licence **at the point in time at which the licence transfers**. An entity shall account for the promise to provide a right to use the entity’s intellectual property as a **performance obligation satisfied at a point in time**. An entity shall apply paragraph **38** to determine the point in time at which the licence transfers to the customer. **However, revenue cannot be recognised for a licence that provides a right to use the entity’s intellectual property before the beginning of the period during which the customer is able to use and benefit from the licence.**\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"38\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If a performance obligation is not satisfied over time in accordance with paragraphs 35–37, an entity satisfies the performance obligation **at a point in time**. To determine the point in time at which a customer obtains control of a promised asset and the entity satisfies a performance obligation, the entity shall consider the requirements for control in paragraphs 31–34. In addition, an entity shall consider indicators of the transfer of control, which include, but are not limited to, the following: **(a) The entity has a present right to payment for the asset** ... **(b) The customer has legal title to the asset** ... **(c) The entity has transferred physical possession of the asset** ... **(d) The customer has the significant risks and rewards of ownership of the asset** ... **(e) The customer has accepted the asset** ...\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778936496119}

cache: keyv:repeat:1:exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py:4606dc2dc0f5c27588a548eb91f4dfc4f2d83a6b2066151c169a0cb3228e57d0:Le traitement du revenu issu d’une licence de propriété intellectuelle dépend de la nature du droit transféré. Quels critères IFRS permettent de choisir entre une reconnaissance ponctuelle et une reconnaissance étalée ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_12-57-25_promptfoo-eval-family-q9"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question porte sur une licence de propriété intellectuelle relevant d’IFRS 15 et demande les critères de choix entre reconnaissance ponctuelle et étalée, sans faits contractuels supplémentaires.\\\",\\n    \\\"Le modèle spécifique des licences n’est appliqué tel quel que si la licence est une obligation de prestation distincte ; sinon, la licence est combinée avec les autres promesses du contrat et le rythme de reconnaissance se détermine au niveau de l’obligation combinée (IFRS 15 B53–B55, 27–30).\\\",\\n    \\\"Aucune analyse spécifique des redevances assises sur les ventes ou l’usage n’est demandée ici ; la réponse vise seulement le choix entre droit d’accès et droit d’utilisation.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Sous IFRS 15, il faut d’abord vérifier si la licence est distincte. Si oui, le revenu est reconnu sur la durée seulement si les critères du droit d’accès sont tous remplis ; sinon, il est reconnu à un moment donné comme droit d’utilisation.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"right_to_access_intellectual_property\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Droit d’accès à la propriété intellectuelle\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, ce traitement ne s’applique que si la licence est distincte puis si la promesse consiste à donner accès à une PI évolutive, et non simplement à remettre un droit figé (IFRS 15 B56). Les trois critères cumulatifs sont : activités attendues de l’entité affectant significativement la PI, exposition directe du client à leurs effets, et absence de transfert d’un bien ou service distinct au fur et à mesure (IFRS 15 B58, B59A). Si ces critères sont remplis, le revenu est reconnu sur la durée (IFRS 15 B60).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"La licence est distincte des autres biens ou services promis dans le contrat.\\\",\\n        \\\"Le contrat impose, ou le client attend raisonnablement, des activités de l’entité qui affectent significativement la PI.\\\",\\n        \\\"Le client est directement exposé aux effets positifs ou négatifs de ces activités.\\\",\\n        \\\"Ces activités ne transfèrent pas séparément un bien ou un service au client au fur et à mesure.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le revenu de licence est reconnu sur la durée, selon une méthode de mesure de l’avancement appropriée.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B56\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the promise to grant the licence is distinct from the other promised goods or services in the contract and, therefore, the promise to grant the licence is a separate performance obligation, an entity shall determine whether the licence transfers to a customer either at a point in time or over time. In making this determination, an entity shall consider whether the nature of the entity’s promise in granting the licence to a customer is to provide the customer with either: **(a)a right to access the entity’s intellectual property as it exists throughout the licence period;** or **(b)a right to use the entity’s intellectual property as it exists at the point in time at which the licence is granted.**\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B58\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The nature of an entity’s promise in granting a licence is a promise to provide a right to access the entity’s intellectual property if **all of the following criteria are met:** **(a)the contract requires, or the customer reasonably expects, that the entity will undertake activities that significantly affect the intellectual property to which the customer has rights** (see paragraphs B59 and B59A); **(b)the rights granted by the licence directly expose the customer to any positive or negative effects of the entity’s activities identified in paragraph B58(a)** ; and **(c)those activities do not result in the transfer of a good or a service to the customer as those activities occur** (see paragraph 25).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B59A\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity’s activities significantly affect the intellectual property to which the customer has rights when either: **(a)those activities are expected to significantly change the form (for example, the design or content) or the functionality (for example, the ability to perform a function or task) of the intellectual property;** or **(b)the ability of the customer to obtain benefit from the intellectual property is substantially derived from, or dependent upon, those activities.** For example, the benefit from a brand is often derived from, or dependent upon, the entity’s ongoing activities that support or maintain the value of the intellectual property.\\\\nAccordingly, if the intellectual property to which the customer has rights has significant stand-alone functionality, a substantial portion of the benefit of that intellectual property is derived from that functionality. Consequently, the ability of the customer to obtain benefit from that intellectual property would not be significantly affected by the entity’s activities unless those activities significantly change its form or functionality. Types of intellectual property that often have significant stand-alone functionality include software, biological compounds or drug formulas, and completed media content (for example, films, television shows and music recordings).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B60\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the criteria in paragraph B58 are met, an entity shall account for the promise to grant a licence as **a performance obligation satisfied over time** because the customer will simultaneously receive and consume the benefit from the entity’s performance of providing access to its intellectual property as the performance occurs (see paragraph 35(a)). An entity shall apply paragraphs 39⁠–⁠45 to select an appropriate method to measure its progress towards complete satisfaction of that performance obligation to provide access.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"right_to_use_intellectual_property\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Droit d’utilisation de la propriété intellectuelle\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, ce traitement s’applique si la licence est distincte mais que les critères du droit d’accès ne sont pas remplis (IFRS 15 B56, B61). La promesse est alors de fournir un droit d’utiliser la PI telle qu’elle existe à la date d’octroi ; le revenu est reconnu à un moment donné, lors du transfert de la licence selon les indicateurs de contrôle d’IFRS 15 38, sans reconnaissance avant que le client puisse effectivement utiliser et tirer avantage de la licence (IFRS 15 B61). Les restrictions de temps, de zone ou d’usage ne changent pas cette conclusion (IFRS 15 B62).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"La licence est distincte des autres biens ou services promis dans le contrat.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les critères cumulatifs du droit d’accès prévus par IFRS 15 B58 ne sont pas remplis.\\\",\\n        \\\"La période d’utilisation ou les moyens permettant l’usage immédiat de la licence ont commencé ou ont été mis à disposition du client.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le revenu de licence est reconnu ponctuellement au moment où le contrôle de la licence est transféré au client.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B56\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the promise to grant the licence is distinct from the other promised goods or services in the contract and, therefore, the promise to grant the licence is a separate performance obligation, an entity shall determine whether the licence transfers to a customer either at a point in time or over time. In making this determination, an entity shall consider whether the nature of the entity’s promise in granting the licence to a customer is to provide the customer with either: **(a)a right to access the entity’s intellectual property as it exists throughout the licence period;** or **(b)a right to use the entity’s intellectual property as it exists at the point in time at which the licence is granted.**\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B61\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the criteria in paragraph B58 are not met, the nature of an entity’s promise is to provide **a right to use the entity’s intellectual property as that intellectual property exists (in terms of form and functionality) at the point in time at which the licence is granted to the customer.** This means that the customer can direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the licence **at the point in time at which the licence transfers.** An entity shall account for the promise to provide a right to use the entity’s intellectual property as **a performance obligation satisfied at a point in time.** An entity shall apply paragraph 38 to determine the point in time at which the licence transfers to the customer. **However, revenue cannot be recognised for a licence that provides a right to use the entity’s intellectual property before the beginning of the period during which the customer is able to use and benefit from the licence.** For example, if a software licence period begins before an entity provides (or otherwise makes available) to the customer a code that enables the customer to immediately use the software, the entity would not recognise revenue before that code has been provided (or otherwise made available).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"38\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If a performance obligation is not satisfied over time in accordance with paragraphs 35⁠–⁠37, an entity satisfies the performance obligation **at a point in time.** To determine the point in time at which a customer obtains control of a promised asset and the entity satisfies a performance obligation, the entity shall consider the requirements for control in paragraphs 31⁠–⁠34. In addition, an entity shall consider indicators of the transfer of control, which include, but are not limited to, the following:\\\\n**(a)The entity has a present right to payment for the asset**—if a customer is presently obliged to pay for an asset, then that may indicate that the customer has obtained the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset in exchange.\\\\n**(b)The customer has legal title to the asset**—legal title may indicate which party to a contract has the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, an asset or to restrict the access of other entities to those benefits. Therefore, the transfer of legal title of an asset may indicate that the customer has obtained control of the asset. If an entity retains legal title solely as protection against the customer’s failure to pay, those rights of the entity would not preclude the customer from obtaining control of an asset.\\\\n**(c)The entity has transferred physical possession of the asset**—the customer’s physical possession of an asset may indicate that the customer has the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset or to restrict the access of other entities to those benefits. However, physical possession may not coincide with control of an asset. For example, in some repurchase agreements and in some consignment arrangements, a customer or consignee may have physical possession of an asset that the entity controls. Conversely, in some bill-and-hold arrangements, the entity may have physical possession of an asset that the customer controls. Paragraphs B64⁠–⁠B76, B77⁠–⁠B78 and B79⁠–⁠B82 provide guidance on accounting for repurchase agreements, consignment arrangements and bill-and-hold arrangements, respectively.\\\\n**(d)The customer has the significant risks and rewards of ownership of the asset**—the transfer of the significant risks and rewards of ownership of an asset to the customer may indicate that the customer has obtained the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset. However, when evaluating the risks and rewards of ownership of a promised asset, an entity shall exclude any risks that give rise to a separate performance obligation in addition to the performance obligation to transfer the asset. For example, an entity may have transferred control of an asset to a customer but not yet satisfied an additional performance obligation to provide maintenance services related to the transferred asset.\\\\n**(e)The customer has accepted the asset**—the customer’s acceptance of an asset may indicate that it has obtained the ability to direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the asset. To evaluate the effect of a contractual customer acceptance clause on when control of an asset is transferred, an entity shall consider the guidance in paragraphs B83⁠–⁠B86.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B62\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall disregard the following factors when determining whether a licence provides a right to access the entity’s intellectual property or a right to use the entity’s intellectual property:\\\\n**(a)Restrictions of time, geographical region or use—those restrictions define the attributes of the promised licence, rather than define whether the entity satisfies its performance obligation at a point in time or over time.**\\\\n**(b)Guarantees provided by the entity that it has a valid patent to intellectual property and that it will defend that patent from unauthorised use**—a promise to defend a patent right is not a performance obligation because the act of defending a patent protects the value of the entity’s intellectual property assets and provides assurance to the customer that the licence transferred meets the specifications of the licence promised in the contract.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778936520776}

cache: keyv:repeat:1:exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py:4606dc2dc0f5c27588a548eb91f4dfc4f2d83a6b2066151c169a0cb3228e57d0:Dans le cadre d’une licence de propriété intellectuelle, faut-il comptabiliser le revenu lorsque le droit est mis à disposition ou l’étaler pendant toute la période d’utilisation autorisée ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_12-57-25_promptfoo-eval-family-q9"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"La question porte sur une licence de propriété intellectuelle relevant d’IFRS 15.\\\",\\n    \\\"Aucun fait ne précise si la licence est distincte d’autres biens ou services; l’analyse ci-dessous suppose que la licence est une obligation de prestation distincte, car c’est le cadre visé par IFRS 15 B56–B61.\\\",\\n    \\\"Aucun fait ne précise si la rémunération comprend une redevance assise sur les ventes ou l’usage; cette question n’est donc pas déterminante pour trancher entre comptabilisation à la mise à disposition ou étalement sur la durée.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Sous IFRS 15, le revenu n’est pas automatiquement reconnu soit à la mise à disposition soit linéairement sur la durée. Il dépend de la nature de la promesse: droit d’accès à la PI sur la période (IFRS 15 B58, B60) ou droit d’utilisation de la PI telle qu’elle existe à l’octroi (IFRS 15 B56, B61).\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"right_to_access\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Droit d’accès\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, l’étalement du revenu pendant la période autorisée s’applique seulement si la licence donne un droit d’accès à la PI tout au long de la période de licence au sens d’IFRS 15 B56. C’est le cas si les trois critères d’IFRS 15 B58 sont remplis; alors le produit est comptabilisé dans le temps selon IFRS 15 B60, car le client reçoit et consomme simultanément l’accès à la PI.\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Le contrat impose, ou le client s’attend raisonnablement à ce que l’entité mène des activités affectant significativement la propriété intellectuelle.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les droits du client l’exposent directement aux effets positifs ou négatifs de ces activités.\\\",\\n        \\\"Ces activités ne donnent pas lieu au transfert d’un bien ou d’un service distinct au fur et à mesure.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le revenu est reconnu sur la durée de la licence, selon une mesure de progression appropriée.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B56\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the promise to grant the licence is distinct from the other promised goods or services in the contract and, therefore, the promise to grant the licence is a separate performance obligation, an entity shall determine whether the licence transfers to a customer either at a point in time or over time. In making this determination, an entity shall consider whether the nature of the entity’s promise in granting the licence to a customer is to provide the customer with either: (a)a **right to access the entity’s intellectual property as it exists throughout the licence period**; or (b)a right to use the entity’s intellectual property as it exists at the point in time at which the licence is granted.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B58\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The nature of an entity’s promise in granting a licence is a promise to provide a **right to access the entity’s intellectual property if all of the following criteria are met**: (a)the contract requires, or the customer reasonably expects, that the entity will undertake activities that **significantly affect the intellectual property** to which the customer has rights (see paragraphs B59 and B59A); (b)the rights granted by the licence directly expose the customer to any positive or negative effects of the entity’s activities identified in paragraph B58(a) ; and (c)those activities do not result in the transfer of a good or a service to the customer as those activities occur (see paragraph 25).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B60\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the criteria in paragraph B58 are met, an entity shall account for the promise to grant a licence as a **performance obligation satisfied over time** because the customer will simultaneously receive and consume the benefit from the entity’s performance of providing access to its intellectual property as the performance occurs (see paragraph 35(a)). An entity shall apply paragraphs 39⁠–⁠45 to select an appropriate method to measure its progress towards complete satisfaction of that performance obligation to provide access.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"right_to_use\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Droit d’utilisation\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, la comptabilisation lorsque le droit est mis à disposition s’applique si les critères du droit d’accès ne sont pas remplis, conformément à IFRS 15 B61. Dans ce cas, la licence donne un droit d’utiliser la PI telle qu’elle existe à la date d’octroi selon IFRS 15 B56, et le produit est reconnu à un moment précis en appliquant les indicateurs de transfert de contrôle d’IFRS 15 38, sans être reconnu avant que le client puisse utiliser et tirer avantage de la licence.\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Les critères d’IFRS 15 B58 pour un droit d’accès ne sont pas remplis.\\\",\\n        \\\"Le client peut effectivement utiliser et tirer avantage de la licence à partir de la date de mise à disposition.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le revenu est reconnu à la date de transfert de la licence, pas sur toute la durée d’utilisation autorisée.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B56\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the promise to grant the licence is distinct from the other promised goods or services in the contract and, therefore, the promise to grant the licence is a separate performance obligation, an entity shall determine whether the licence transfers to a customer either at a point in time or over time. In making this determination, an entity shall consider whether the nature of the entity’s promise in granting the licence to a customer is to provide the customer with either: (a)a right to access the entity’s intellectual property as it exists throughout the licence period; or (b)a **right to use the entity’s intellectual property as it exists at the point in time at which the licence is granted**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B61\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If the criteria in paragraph B58 are not met, the nature of an entity’s promise is to provide a **right to use the entity’s intellectual property as that intellectual property exists (in terms of form and functionality) at the point in time at which the licence is granted to the customer**. This means that the customer can direct the use of, and obtain substantially all of the remaining benefits from, the licence at the point in time at which the licence transfers. An entity shall account for the promise to provide a right to use the entity’s intellectual property as a **performance obligation satisfied at a point in time**. An entity shall apply paragraph 38 to determine the point in time at which the licence transfers to the customer. However, **revenue cannot be recognised** for a licence that provides a right to use the entity’s intellectual property **before the beginning of the period during which the customer is able to use and benefit from the licence**. For example, if a software licence period begins before an entity provides (or otherwise makes available) to the customer a code that enables the customer to immediately use the software, the entity would not recognise revenue before that code has been provided (or otherwise made available).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs15\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"38\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If a performance obligation is not satisfied over time in accordance with paragraphs 35⁠–⁠37, an entity satisfies the performance obligation **at a point in time**. To determine the point in time at which a customer obtains control of a promised asset and the entity satisfies a performance obligation, the entity shall consider the requirements for control in paragraphs 31⁠–⁠34. In addition, an entity shall consider indicators of the transfer of control, which include, but are not limited to, the following: (a)The entity has a **present right to payment** for the asset... (b)The customer has **legal title** to the asset... (c)The entity has transferred **physical possession** of the asset... (d)The customer has the **significant risks and rewards of ownership** of the asset... (e)The customer has **accepted the asset**...\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778936550623}

cache: keyv:repeat:0:exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py:4606dc2dc0f5c27588a548eb91f4dfc4f2d83a6b2066151c169a0cb3228e57d0:Un contrat (sans option d'achat) d'une durée inférieure ou égale à un an renouvelable par tacite reconduction ou à durée indéterminée peut-il être qualifié de contrat de courte durée pour lequel le preneur peut bénéficier de l'exemption optionnelle de reconnaissance du droit d'utilisation et de la dette de loyers ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_13-02-32_promptfoo-eval-family-q12"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"Le contrat ne comporte pas d’option d’achat, conformément aux faits décrits.\\\",\\n    \\\"La question porte sur le point de vue du preneur et sur l’éligibilité à l’exemption optionnelle de court terme à la date de commencement.\\\",\\n    \\\"Pour un contrat renouvelable tacitement ou à durée indéterminée, la durée du bail est appréciée selon la période exécutoire et, le cas échéant, selon les options de prolongation ou de résiliation que le preneur est raisonnablement certain d’exercer ou de ne pas exercer.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Oui, mais seulement si la durée du bail au sens d’IFRS 16 n’excède pas 12 mois à la date de commencement. Un bail renouvelable tacitement ou à durée indéterminée ne peut pas être qualifié automatiquement de courte durée : tout dépend de sa période exécutoire et de l’appréciation de la durée du bail.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"expense_recognition_for_short_term_leases\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Exemption optionnelle pour bail de courte durée\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette approche n’est applicable dans cette situation que si le bail est, en substance, un bail de courte durée au sens d’IFRS 16, c’est-à-dire si la durée du bail déterminée selon IFRS 16 18 n’excède pas 12 mois et qu’il n’existe pas d’option d’achat (IFRS 16 5, IFRS 16 18). Pour un bail renouvelable tacitement ou à durée indéterminée, il faut d’abord apprécier la période exécutoire du contrat; si le contrat reste exécutoire au-delà de 12 mois ou si le preneur est raisonnablement certain de prolonger ou de ne pas résilier, l’exemption ne s’applique pas (IFRS 16 B34, IFRS 16 B35).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"La durée du bail, déterminée à la date de commencement, ne dépasse pas 12 mois.\\\",\\n        \\\"La période exécutoire du contrat n’étend pas la durée du bail au-delà de 12 mois.\\\",\\n        \\\"Le preneur n’est pas raisonnablement certain d’exercer une prolongation ni de ne pas exercer une faculté de résiliation qui porterait la durée au-delà de 12 mois.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si ces conditions sont remplies et si le preneur choisit l’exemption, les loyers sont comptabilisés en charge et non via un droit d’utilisation et une dette.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"5\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A lessee may elect not to apply the requirements in paragraphs 22⁠–⁠49 to: (a)**short-term leases** ; and (b)leases for which the underlying asset is of low value (as described in paragraphs B3⁠–⁠B8).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"18\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall determine the lease term as the **non-cancellable period of a lease**, together with both: (a)periods covered by an option to extend the lease if the lessee is **reasonably certain to exercise that option**; and (b)periods covered by an option to terminate the lease if the lessee is **reasonably certain not to exercise that option**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B34\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In determining the lease term and assessing the length of the non-cancellable period of a lease, an entity shall apply the definition of a contract and determine the **period for which the contract is enforceable**. A lease is no longer enforceable when the lessee and the lessor each has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with **no more than an insignificant penalty**. E10\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B35\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If only a lessee has the right to terminate a lease, that right is considered to be an option to terminate the lease available to the lessee that an entity considers when determining the lease term. If only a lessor has the right to terminate a lease, the **non-cancellable period of the lease includes the period covered by the option to terminate the lease**.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"recognition_of_a_right_of_use_asset_and_lease_liability\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Modèle général de comptabilisation du preneur\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette approche s’applique dans cette situation dès lors que le contrat n’est pas éligible à l’exemption de court terme, ce qui sera le cas si la durée du bail, déterminée selon IFRS 16 18, excède 12 mois du fait de la période exécutoire ou de l’appréciation des options de prolongation/résiliation (IFRS 16 18, IFRS 16 B34). Dans ce cas, le preneur doit reconnaître un droit d’utilisation et une dette de loyers à la date de commencement (IFRS 16 22, IFRS 16 26).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"La durée du bail au sens d’IFRS 16 dépasse 12 mois.\\\",\\n        \\\"Ou bien la période exécutoire et les droits de résiliation/prolongation conduisent à exclure la qualification de bail de courte durée.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le preneur comptabilise un droit d’utilisation et une dette de loyers, puis mesure la dette selon le modèle ultérieur d’IFRS 16.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"18\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall determine the lease term as the **non-cancellable period of a lease**, together with both: (a)periods covered by an option to extend the lease if the lessee is **reasonably certain to exercise that option**; and (b)periods covered by an option to terminate the lease if the lessee is **reasonably certain not to exercise that option**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B34\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In determining the lease term and assessing the length of the non-cancellable period of a lease, an entity shall apply the definition of a contract and determine the **period for which the contract is enforceable**. A lease is no longer enforceable when the lessee and the lessor each has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with **no more than an insignificant penalty**. E10\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"22\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"At the commencement date, a lessee shall **recognise a right-of-use asset and a lease liability**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"26\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"At the commencement date, a lessee shall measure the lease liability at the **present value of the lease payments** that are not paid at that date. The lease payments shall be discounted using the interest rate implicit in the lease, if that rate can be readily determined. If that rate cannot be readily determined, the lessee shall use the lessee’s incremental borrowing rate.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778936620411}

cache: keyv:repeat:1:exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py:4606dc2dc0f5c27588a548eb91f4dfc4f2d83a6b2066151c169a0cb3228e57d0:Un contrat (sans option d'achat) d'une durée inférieure ou égale à un an renouvelable par tacite reconduction ou à durée indéterminée peut-il être qualifié de contrat de courte durée pour lequel le preneur peut bénéficier de l'exemption optionnelle de reconnaissance du droit d'utilisation et de la dette de loyers ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_13-02-32_promptfoo-eval-family-q12"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"Le contrat ne comporte pas d’option d’achat, comme indiqué dans la question.\\\",\\n    \\\"L’analyse porte sur la position du preneur et sur l’exemption optionnelle prévue par IFRS 16.\\\",\\n    \\\"La qualification de « courte durée » dépend de la lease term déterminée selon la période exécutoire du contrat et non de la seule durée faciale initiale.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Oui, mais seulement si la lease term effectivement déterminée selon IFRS 16 n’excède pas 12 mois. Un bail renouvelable par tacite reconduction ou à durée indéterminée n’est pas automatiquement de courte durée : il faut d’abord apprécier la période exécutoire et, le cas échéant, les options de prolongation/termination.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"short_term_lease_exemption\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Exemption pour les contrats de courte durée\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"L’exemption optionnelle n’est disponible que pour un contrat qualifié de courte durée au sens d’IFRS 16 5, et les loyers sont alors comptabilisés en charges selon IFRS 16 6. Pour un contrat <= 1 an renouvelable tacitement ou à durée indéterminée, il faut d’abord déterminer la lease term selon IFRS 16 18 et la période exécutoire selon IFRS 16 B34-B35 ; si le contrat reste exécutoire au-delà de 12 mois ou si le preneur est raisonnablement certain de prolonger / de ne pas résilier, l’exemption ne s’applique pas.\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"La lease term déterminée selon IFRS 16 ne dépasse pas 12 mois.\\\",\\n        \\\"La période exécutoire du contrat n’étend pas le bail au-delà de 12 mois.\\\",\\n        \\\"Le preneur n’est pas raisonnablement certain d’exercer une prolongation ou de ne pas exercer une option de résiliation conduisant à une lease term > 12 mois.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si ces conditions sont remplies et si l’entité choisit l’exemption, les paiements de loyers sont comptabilisés en charges sans comptabiliser de droit d’utilisation ni de dette locative.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"5\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A lessee may elect not to apply the requirements in paragraphs 22⁠–⁠49 to: **(a)short-term leases**; and **(b)leases for which the underlying asset is of low value** (as described in paragraphs B3⁠–⁠B8).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"6\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If a lessee elects not to apply the requirements in paragraphs 22⁠–⁠49 to either short-term leases or leases for which the underlying asset is of low value, the lessee shall **recognise the lease payments associated with those leases as an expense** on either a straight-line basis over the lease term or another systematic basis. The lessee shall apply another systematic basis if that basis is more representative of the pattern of the lessee’s benefit.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"18\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall determine the lease term as **the non-cancellable period of a lease**, together with both: **(a)periods covered by an option to extend the lease if the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise that option; and (b)periods covered by an option to terminate the lease if the lessee is reasonably certain not to exercise that option**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"19\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In assessing whether a lessee is reasonably certain to exercise an option to extend a lease, or not to exercise an option to terminate a lease, an entity shall **consider all relevant facts and circumstances that create an economic incentive** for the lessee to exercise the option to extend the lease, or not to exercise the option to terminate the lease, as described in paragraphs B37⁠–⁠B40.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B34\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In determining the lease term and assessing the length of the non-cancellable period of a lease, an entity shall apply the definition of a contract and determine **the period for which the contract is enforceable**. A lease is no longer enforceable when **the lessee and the lessor each has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty**. E10\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B35\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If only a lessee has the right to terminate a lease, that right is considered to be **an option to terminate the lease available to the lessee that an entity considers when determining the lease term**. If only a lessor has the right to terminate a lease, **the non-cancellable period of the lease includes the period covered by the option to terminate the lease**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B37\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"At the commencement date, an entity assesses whether the lessee is **reasonably certain to exercise an option to extend the lease** or to purchase the underlying asset, or **not to exercise an option to terminate the lease**. The entity considers **all relevant facts and circumstances that create an economic incentive** for the lessee to exercise, or not to exercise, the option, including any expected changes in facts and circumstances from the commencement date until the exercise date of the option.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B39\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"**The shorter the non-cancellable period of a lease, the more likely a lessee is to exercise an option to extend the lease or not to exercise an option to terminate the lease.** This is because the costs associated with obtaining a replacement asset are likely to be proportionately higher the shorter the non-cancellable period.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"right_of_use_asset_and_lease_liability\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Modèle général avec droit d’utilisation et dette locative\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Ce modèle s’applique dès la date de commencement si l’exemption de courte durée n’est pas disponible ou n’est pas retenue par le preneur (IFRS 16 22). Donc, pour un bail renouvelable tacitement ou à durée indéterminée, si la lease term excède 12 mois au regard d’IFRS 16 18 et B34-B35, le preneur doit comptabiliser un droit d’utilisation et une dette de loyers selon IFRS 16 22, 26 et 29-30.\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"La lease term déterminée selon IFRS 16 excède 12 mois.\\\",\\n        \\\"Ou bien l’entité n’exerce pas l’exemption optionnelle alors même qu’elle serait disponible.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"À défaut d’exemption applicable et choisie, le preneur comptabilise à l’origine un droit d’utilisation et une dette locative, puis comptabilise amortissement et intérêts.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"18\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall determine the lease term as **the non-cancellable period of a lease**, together with both: **(a)periods covered by an option to extend the lease if the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise that option; and (b)periods covered by an option to terminate the lease if the lessee is reasonably certain not to exercise that option**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"22\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"At the commencement date, a lessee shall **recognise a right-of-use asset and a lease liability**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"26\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"At the commencement date, a lessee shall **measure the lease liability at the present value of the lease payments that are not paid at that date**. The lease payments shall be discounted using the interest rate implicit in the lease, if that rate can be readily determined. If that rate cannot be readily determined, the lessee shall use the lessee’s incremental borrowing rate.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"29\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"After the commencement date, a lessee shall **measure the right-of-use asset applying a cost model**, unless it applies either of the measurement models described in paragraphs 34 and 35.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"30\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"To apply a cost model, a lessee shall measure the right‑of‑use asset at cost: **(a)less any accumulated depreciation and any accumulated impairment losses; and (b)adjusted for any remeasurement of the lease liability** specified in paragraph 36(c).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B34\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In determining the lease term and assessing the length of the non-cancellable period of a lease, an entity shall apply the definition of a contract and determine **the period for which the contract is enforceable**. A lease is no longer enforceable when **the lessee and the lessor each has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty**. E10\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B35\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If only a lessee has the right to terminate a lease, that right is considered to be **an option to terminate the lease available to the lessee that an entity considers when determining the lease term**. If only a lessor has the right to terminate a lease, **the non-cancellable period of the lease includes the period covered by the option to terminate the lease**.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778936631123}

cache: keyv:repeat:0:exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py:4606dc2dc0f5c27588a548eb91f4dfc4f2d83a6b2066151c169a0cb3228e57d0:Un contrat de location sans option d’achat, conclu pour une durée d’un an ou moins mais renouvelable tacitement ou à durée indéterminée, peut-il entrer dans l’exemption IFRS 16 applicable aux contrats de courte durée ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_13-02-32_promptfoo-eval-family-q12"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"Le contrat ne comporte pas d’option d’achat, conformément aux faits décrits.\\\",\\n    \\\"La question porte sur un preneur appliquant IFRS 16 et sur l’éligibilité à l’exemption des contrats de courte durée.\\\",\\n    \\\"Aucune autre exemption n’est analysée ici, notamment l’exemption relative aux actifs de faible valeur.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Oui, mais seulement si la durée de location déterminée selon la période exécutoire et le terme du bail n’excède pas 12 mois. Si le renouvellement tacite ou la durée indéterminée conduit à une durée exécutoire au-delà de 12 mois, l’exemption courte durée ne s’applique pas et le modèle général IFRS 16 s’impose.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"short_term_lease_exemption\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Exemption des contrats de courte durée\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"L’exemption est permise seulement pour les contrats de courte durée (IFRS 16 5), ce qui suppose d’abord de déterminer le terme du bail à partir de la période exécutoire (IFRS 16 B34, E10). Dans un contrat d’un an ou moins mais renouvelable tacitement ou à durée indéterminée, l’exemption n’est possible que si le bail cesse d’être exécutoire au plus tard dans les 12 mois; sinon, la durée de location dépasse la courte durée, même sans option d’achat (IFRS 16 B35, B37).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"La durée de location déterminée selon la période exécutoire ne dépasse pas 12 mois.\\\",\\n        \\\"Chaque partie peut mettre fin au contrat sans permission de l’autre avec une pénalité n’excédant pas un montant insignifiant, ou les faits et circonstances ne rendent pas le bail exécutoire au-delà de 12 mois.\\\",\\n        \\\"Le preneur élit effectivement l’exemption de courte durée pour la classe d’actifs concernée.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si ces conditions sont remplies, les paiements de loyers sont comptabilisés en charge, en ligne droite ou selon une autre base systématique.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"5\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A lessee may elect not to apply the requirements in paragraphs 22⁠–⁠49 to: (a)**short-term leases** ; and (b)leases for which the underlying asset is of low value (as described in paragraphs B3⁠–⁠B8).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"6\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If a lessee elects not to apply the requirements in paragraphs 22⁠–⁠49 to either short-term leases or leases for which the underlying asset is of low value, the lessee shall **recognise the lease payments associated with those leases as an expense** on either a straight-line basis over the lease term or another systematic basis. The lessee shall apply another systematic basis if that basis is more representative of the pattern of the lessee’s benefit.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"8\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The election for short-term leases shall be made **by class of underlying asset** to which the right of use relates. A class of underlying asset is a grouping of underlying assets of a similar nature and use in an entity’s operations. The election for leases for which the underlying asset is of low value can be made on a lease-by-lease basis.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B34\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In determining the lease term and assessing the length of the non-cancellable period of a lease, an entity shall apply the definition of a contract and determine **the period for which the contract is enforceable**. A lease is no longer enforceable when the lessee and the lessor each has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with **no more than an insignificant penalty**. E10\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B35\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If only a lessee has the right to terminate a lease, that right is considered to be an option to terminate the lease available to the lessee that an entity considers when determining the lease term. If only a lessor has the right to terminate a lease, the non-cancellable period of the lease **includes the period covered by the option to terminate the lease**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B37\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"At the commencement date, an entity assesses whether the lessee is **reasonably certain** to exercise an option to extend the lease or to purchase the underlying asset, or **not to exercise an option to terminate the lease**. The entity considers all relevant facts and circumstances that create an economic incentive for the lessee to exercise, or not to exercise, the option, including any expected changes in facts and circumstances from the commencement date until the exercise date of the option.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"E10\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"[IFRIC® Update, November 2019, Agenda Decision, ‘ IFRS 16 Leases and IAS 16 Property, Plant and Equipment —Lease Term and Useful Life of Leasehold Improvements’ + The Committee observed that, in applying paragraph B34 and determining the enforceable period of the lease described in the request, an entity considers: (a)**the broader economics of the contract, and not only contractual termination payments**. For example, if either party has an economic incentive not to terminate the lease such that it would incur a penalty on termination that is **more than insignificant, the contract is enforceable beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated**; and (b)whether each of the parties has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty. Applying paragraph B34, **a lease is no longer enforceable only when both parties have such a right**. Consequently, if only one party has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty, the contract is enforceable beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated by that party. If an entity concludes that the contract is enforceable beyond the notice period of a cancellable lease (or the initial period of a renewable lease), it then applies paragraphs 19 and B37⁠–⁠B40 of IFRS 16 to assess whether the lessee is reasonably certain not to exercise the option to terminate the lease.]\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"right_of_use_asset_and_lease_liability\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Modèle général avec actif de droit d’utilisation et dette locative\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Si le contrat renouvelable tacitement ou à durée indéterminée ne remplit pas l’exemption courte durée, le preneur applique le modèle général et comptabilise un actif de droit d’utilisation et une dette locative à la date de commencement (IFRS 16 22). C’est le cas lorsque la période exécutoire ou l’appréciation des options de terminaison/renouvellement conduit à une durée de location supérieure à 12 mois (IFRS 16 B34, E10, B37).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"Le contrat n’est pas éligible à l’exemption de courte durée parce que la durée de location excède 12 mois.\\\",\\n        \\\"Ou bien le preneur n’a pas élu l’exemption de courte durée pour la classe d’actifs concernée.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le preneur doit reconnaître à l’origine un actif de droit d’utilisation et une dette locative.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"22\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"At the commencement date, a lessee shall **recognise a right-of-use asset and a lease liability**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B34\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In determining the lease term and assessing the length of the non-cancellable period of a lease, an entity shall apply the definition of a contract and determine **the period for which the contract is enforceable**. A lease is no longer enforceable when the lessee and the lessor each has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with **no more than an insignificant penalty**. E10\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B37\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"At the commencement date, an entity assesses whether the lessee is **reasonably certain** to exercise an option to extend the lease or to purchase the underlying asset, or **not to exercise an option to terminate the lease**. The entity considers all relevant facts and circumstances that create an economic incentive for the lessee to exercise, or not to exercise, the option, including any expected changes in facts and circumstances from the commencement date until the exercise date of the option.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"E10\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"[IFRIC® Update, November 2019, Agenda Decision, ‘ IFRS 16 Leases and IAS 16 Property, Plant and Equipment —Lease Term and Useful Life of Leasehold Improvements’ + The Committee observed that, in applying paragraph B34 and determining the enforceable period of the lease described in the request, an entity considers: (a)**the broader economics of the contract, and not only contractual termination payments**. For example, if either party has an economic incentive not to terminate the lease such that it would incur a penalty on termination that is **more than insignificant, the contract is enforceable beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated**; and (b)whether each of the parties has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty. Applying paragraph B34, **a lease is no longer enforceable only when both parties have such a right**. Consequently, if only one party has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty, the contract is enforceable beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated by that party. If an entity concludes that the contract is enforceable beyond the notice period of a cancellable lease (or the initial period of a renewable lease), it then applies paragraphs 19 and B37⁠–⁠B40 of IFRS 16 to assess whether the lessee is reasonably certain not to exercise the option to terminate the lease.]\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778936634263}
