Peut-il ne pas reconnaître le droit d’utilisation et la dette de loyers au titre de l’exemption pour contrats courts ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_13-02-32_promptfoo-eval-family-q12"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"Le contrat ne comporte pas d’option d’achat, conformément aux faits donnés.\\\",\\n    \\\"La question porte sur la comptabilisation du preneur à la date de commencement du contrat.\\\",\\n    \\\"Aucune autre exemption d’IFRS 16 n’est analysée ici, seule l’exemption relative aux contrats courts est considérée.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"Le preneur peut appliquer l’exemption pour contrats courts seulement si la durée de location déterminée selon IFRS 16 n’excède pas 12 mois. En présence d’une tacite reconduction ou d’une durée indéterminée, il faut d’abord apprécier la période exécutoire et, le cas échéant, la certitude raisonnable de ne pas mettre fin au bail.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"short_term_lease_exemption\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Exemption contrats courts\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"L’exemption n’est disponible que pour un contrat dont la durée de location n’excède pas 12 mois et sans option d’achat (IFRS 16 5). Or, avec une tacite reconduction ou une durée indéterminée, la durée ne se limite pas automatiquement à la période initiale: il faut déterminer la période exécutoire (IFRS 16 B34, B35) puis la durée de location incluant les périodes que le preneur est raisonnablement certain de prolonger ou de ne pas résilier (IFRS 16 18, 19, B37, B39; IFRS 16 E10). Si cette durée ainsi déterminée reste au plus égale à 12 mois, le preneur peut ne pas reconnaître de droit d’utilisation ni de dette et comptabilise les loyers en charge (IFRS 16 6).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"La durée de location déterminée selon IFRS 16, en tenant compte de la période exécutoire, n’excède pas 12 mois.\\\",\\n        \\\"L’analyse de la tacite reconduction ou de la durée indéterminée ne conduit pas à une période exécutoire au-delà de 12 mois ou à une certitude raisonnable de ne pas résilier au-delà de 12 mois.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si ces conditions sont remplies, les paiements de loyers sont comptabilisés en charge, sans reconnaissance initiale d’un droit d’utilisation ni d’une dette.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"5\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A lessee may elect not to apply the requirements in paragraphs 22⁠–⁠49 to: (a)**short-term leases** ; and (b)leases for which the underlying asset is of low value (as described in paragraphs B3⁠–⁠B8).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"6\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If a lessee elects not to apply the requirements in paragraphs 22⁠–⁠49 to either short-term leases or leases for which the underlying asset is of low value, the lessee shall **recognise the lease payments associated with those leases as an expense** on either a straight-line basis over the lease term or another systematic basis. The lessee shall apply another systematic basis if that basis is more representative of the pattern of the lessee’s benefit.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"18\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall determine the lease term as the **non-cancellable period of a lease**, together with both: (a)periods covered by an option to extend the lease if the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise that option; and (b)periods covered by an option to terminate the lease if the lessee is reasonably certain not to exercise that option.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"19\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In assessing whether a lessee is reasonably certain to exercise an option to extend a lease, or not to exercise an option to terminate a lease, an entity shall consider **all relevant facts and circumstances that create an economic incentive** for the lessee to exercise the option to extend the lease, or not to exercise the option to terminate the lease, as described in paragraphs B37⁠–⁠B40.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B34\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In determining the lease term and assessing the length of the non-cancellable period of a lease, an entity shall apply the definition of a contract and determine the **period for which the contract is enforceable**. A lease is no longer enforceable when the lessee and the lessor each has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with **no more than an insignificant penalty**. E10\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B35\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If only a lessee has the right to terminate a lease, that right is considered to be an option to terminate the lease available to the lessee that an entity considers when determining the lease term. If only a lessor has the right to terminate a lease, the **non-cancellable period of the lease includes the period covered by the option to terminate the lease**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B37\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"At the commencement date, an entity assesses whether the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise an option to extend the lease or to purchase the underlying asset, or **not to exercise an option to terminate the lease**. The entity considers **all relevant facts and circumstances that create an economic incentive** for the lessee to exercise, or not to exercise, the option, including any expected changes in facts and circumstances from the commencement date until the exercise date of the option.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B39\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The **shorter the non-cancellable period of a lease, the more likely a lessee is to exercise an option to extend the lease or not to exercise an option to terminate the lease**. This is because the costs associated with obtaining a replacement asset are likely to be proportionately higher the shorter the non-cancellable period.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"E10\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"[IFRIC® Update, November 2019, Agenda Decision, ‘ IFRS 16 Leases and IAS 16 Property, Plant and Equipment —Lease Term and Useful Life of Leasehold Improvements’ + The Committee received a request about cancellable or renewable leases. The cancellable lease described in the request is one that does not specify a particular contractual term but continues indefinitely until either party to the contract gives notice to terminate. The contract includes a notice period of, for example, less than 12 months and the contract does not oblige either party to make a payment on termination. The renewable lease described in the request is one that specifies an initial period, and renews indefinitely at the end of the initial period unless terminated by either of the parties to the contract. ... The Committee observed that, in applying paragraph B34 and determining the enforceable period of the lease described in the request, an entity considers: (a)**the broader economics of the contract, and not only contractual termination payments**. For example, if either party has an economic incentive not to terminate the lease such that it would incur a penalty on termination that is more than insignificant, the contract is enforceable beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated; and (b)**whether each of the parties has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty**. Applying paragraph B34, a lease is no longer enforceable only when both parties have such a right. Consequently, if only one party has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty, the contract is enforceable beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated by that party. If an entity concludes that the contract is enforceable beyond the notice period of a cancellable lease (or the initial period of a renewable lease), it then applies paragraphs 19 and B37⁠–⁠B40 of IFRS 16 to assess whether the lessee is reasonably certain not to exercise the option to terminate the lease. …]\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"right_of_use_model\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Modèle général droit d’utilisation\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"À défaut de satisfaire à l’exemption contrats courts, le preneur doit comptabiliser un droit d’utilisation et une dette de loyers à la date de commencement (IFRS 16 22). Dans cette situation, ce traitement s’applique si la période exécutoire et la durée de location, appréciées au regard de la tacite reconduction ou de la durée indéterminée, dépassent 12 mois ou si le preneur est raisonnablement certain de ne pas résilier au-delà de 12 mois (IFRS 16 18, 19, B34, B37; IFRS 16 E10). Le simple fait que la période initiale soit inférieure ou égale à 12 mois n’est donc pas suffisant en soi pour éviter la reconnaissance.\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"La durée de location déterminée selon IFRS 16 excède 12 mois.\\\",\\n        \\\"Ou bien l’analyse de la période exécutoire montre que le contrat reste exécutoire au-delà de la période initiale ou du préavis.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si ces conditions sont remplies, le preneur reconnaît le droit d’utilisation et la dette de loyers au commencement.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"22\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"At the commencement date, a lessee shall **recognise a right-of-use asset and a lease liability**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"18\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall determine the lease term as the **non-cancellable period of a lease**, together with both: (a)periods covered by an option to extend the lease if the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise that option; and (b)periods covered by an option to terminate the lease if the lessee is reasonably certain not to exercise that option.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"19\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In assessing whether a lessee is reasonably certain to exercise an option to extend a lease, or not to exercise an option to terminate a lease, an entity shall consider **all relevant facts and circumstances that create an economic incentive** for the lessee to exercise the option to extend the lease, or not to exercise the option to terminate the lease, as described in paragraphs B37⁠–⁠B40.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B34\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In determining the lease term and assessing the length of the non-cancellable period of a lease, an entity shall apply the definition of a contract and determine the **period for which the contract is enforceable**. A lease is no longer enforceable when the lessee and the lessor each has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with **no more than an insignificant penalty**. E10\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B37\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"At the commencement date, an entity assesses whether the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise an option to extend the lease or to purchase the underlying asset, or **not to exercise an option to terminate the lease**. The entity considers **all relevant facts and circumstances that create an economic incentive** for the lessee to exercise, or not to exercise, the option, including any expected changes in facts and circumstances from the commencement date until the exercise date of the option.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"E10\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"[IFRIC® Update, November 2019, Agenda Decision, ‘ IFRS 16 Leases and IAS 16 Property, Plant and Equipment —Lease Term and Useful Life of Leasehold Improvements’ + The Committee received a request about cancellable or renewable leases. The cancellable lease described in the request is one that does not specify a particular contractual term but continues indefinitely until either party to the contract gives notice to terminate. The contract includes a notice period of, for example, less than 12 months and the contract does not oblige either party to make a payment on termination. The renewable lease described in the request is one that specifies an initial period, and renews indefinitely at the end of the initial period unless terminated by either of the parties to the contract. ... The Committee observed that, in applying paragraph B34 and determining the enforceable period of the lease described in the request, an entity considers: (a)**the broader economics of the contract, and not only contractual termination payments**. For example, if either party has an economic incentive not to terminate the lease such that it would incur a penalty on termination that is more than insignificant, the contract is enforceable beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated; and (b)**whether each of the parties has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty**. Applying paragraph B34, a lease is no longer enforceable only when both parties have such a right. Consequently, if only one party has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty, the contract is enforceable beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated by that party. If an entity concludes that the contract is enforceable beyond the notice period of a cancellable lease (or the initial period of a renewable lease), it then applies paragraphs 19 and B37⁠–⁠B40 of IFRS 16 to assess whether the lessee is reasonably certain not to exercise the option to terminate the lease. …]\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778936845376}

cache: keyv:repeat:0:exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py:4606dc2dc0f5c27588a548eb91f4dfc4f2d83a6b2066151c169a0cb3228e57d0:Pour un contrat sans option d’achat renouvelable par tacite reconduction, ou conclu sans terme défini, comment apprécier si la durée de location permet l’application de l’exemption de courte durée ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_13-02-32_promptfoo-eval-family-q12"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"Le contrat ne comporte pas d’option d’achat, conformément à la question.\\\",\\n    \\\"La question porte sur l’éligibilité du preneur à l’exemption de courte durée d’IFRS 16.\\\",\\n    \\\"Aucun fait complémentaire n’est fourni sur les pénalités économiques, les aménagements significatifs, ni l’importance opérationnelle de l’actif ; l’analyse est donc conditionnée par l’appréciation de la durée exécutoire et de la certitude raisonnable de renouveler ou de ne pas résilier.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"L’exemption de courte durée peut s’appliquer seulement si, après détermination de la durée de location selon la période exécutoire et les renouvellements/résiliations raisonnablement certains, la durée retenue n’excède pas 12 mois (IFRS 16 5, 18, B34, E10). Si la tacite reconduction ou l’absence de terme conduit à une durée exécutoire plus longue, le modèle général preneur s’applique.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"short_term_lease_exemption\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Exemption de courte durée\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Dans cette situation, l’exemption n’est pas appréciée sur la seule base du préavis contractuel ou de l’absence de terme ; il faut d’abord déterminer la durée de location au sens d’IFRS 16, c’est-à-dire la période non résiliable plus les périodes couvertes par des options que le preneur est raisonnablement certain d’exercer ou de ne pas exercer (IFRS 16 18, 19). Pour un bail à tacite reconduction ou sans terme défini, il faut aussi apprécier la période exécutoire en tenant compte de l’économie globale du contrat et du fait que le bail n’est plus exécutoire que lorsque les deux parties peuvent y mettre fin sans pénalité plus qu’insignifiante (IFRS 16 B34, E10). L’exemption n’est donc disponible que si cette durée ainsi déterminée est de courte durée au sens d’IFRS 16 5.\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"La durée de location déterminée selon IFRS 16 18 et B34 n’excède pas 12 mois.\\\",\\n        \\\"Les faits et circonstances ne montrent pas que le preneur est raisonnablement certain de prolonger le bail ou de ne pas le résilier au-delà de cette période.\\\",\\n        \\\"L’analyse de la période exécutoire, y compris les pénalités économiques au sens large, ne conduit pas à retenir une durée supérieure.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si ces conditions sont remplies, les paiements de loyers sont comptabilisés en charge selon IFRS 16 6, sans appliquer les paragraphes 22 à 49.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"5\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A lessee may elect not to apply the requirements in paragraphs 22⁠–⁠49 to: (a)**short-term leases**; and (b)leases for which the underlying asset is of low value (as described in paragraphs B3⁠–⁠B8).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"18\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall determine the lease term as **the non-cancellable period of a lease**, together with both: (a)periods covered by an option to extend the lease if the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise that option; and (b)periods covered by an option to terminate the lease if the lessee is reasonably certain not to exercise that option.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"19\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In assessing whether a lessee is reasonably certain to exercise an option to extend a lease, or not to exercise an option to terminate a lease, an entity shall consider **all relevant facts and circumstances that create an economic incentive** for the lessee to exercise the option to extend the lease, or not to exercise the option to terminate the lease, as described in paragraphs B37⁠–⁠B40.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B34\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In determining the lease term and assessing the length of the non-cancellable period of a lease, an entity shall apply the definition of a contract and determine **the period for which the contract is enforceable**. A lease is no longer enforceable when the lessee and the lessor each has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with **no more than an insignificant penalty**. E10\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"E10\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"[IFRIC® Update, November 2019, Agenda Decision, ‘ IFRS 16 Leases and IAS 16 Property, Plant and Equipment —Lease Term and Useful Life of Leasehold Improvements’ + The Committee received a request about cancellable or renewable leases. The cancellable lease described in the request is one that does not specify a particular contractual term but continues indefinitely until either party to the contract gives notice to terminate. The contract includes a notice period of, for example, less than 12 months and the contract does not oblige either party to make a payment on termination. The renewable lease described in the request is one that specifies an initial period, and renews indefinitely at the end of the initial period unless terminated by either of the parties to the contract. The request asked two questions: (a)how to determine the lease term of a cancellable lease or a renewable lease. Specifically, the request asked whether, when applying paragraph B34 of IFRS 16 and assessing ‘no more than an insignificant penalty’, an entity considers the broader economics of the contract, and not only contractual termination payments. Such considerations might include, for example, the cost of abandoning or dismantling leasehold improvements. (b)whether the useful life of any related non-removable leasehold improvements is limited to the lease term determined applying IFRS 16. Non-removable leasehold improvements are, for example, fixtures and fittings acquired by the lessee and constructed on the underlying asset that is the subject of the cancellable or renewable lease. The lessee will use and benefit from the leasehold improvements only for as long as it uses the underlying asset. Lease term Paragraph 18 of IFRS 16 requires an entity to determine the lease term as the non-cancellable period of a lease, together with both (a) periods covered by an option to extend the lease if the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise that option; and (b) periods covered by an option to terminate the lease if the lessee is reasonably certain not to exercise that option. In determining the lease term and assessing the length of the non-cancellable period of a lease, paragraph B34 of IFRS 16 requires an entity to determine **the period for which the contract is enforceable**. Paragraph B34 specifies that ‘a lease is no longer enforceable when the lessee and the lessor each has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with **no more than an insignificant penalty**’. Paragraph BC156 sets out the Board’s view that ‘the lease term should reflect an entity’s reasonable expectation of the period during which the underlying asset will be used because that approach provides the most useful information’. The Committee observed that, in applying paragraph B34 and determining the enforceable period of the lease described in the request, an entity considers: **(a)the broader economics of the contract, and not only contractual termination payments**. For example, if either party has an economic incentive not to terminate the lease such that it would incur a penalty on termination that is more than insignificant, the contract is enforceable beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated; and (b)whether each of the parties has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty. Applying paragraph B34, **a lease is no longer enforceable only when both parties have such a right**. Consequently, if only one party has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty, the contract is enforceable beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated by that party. If an entity concludes that the contract is enforceable beyond the notice period of a cancellable lease (or the initial period of a renewable lease), it then applies paragraphs 19 and B37⁠–⁠B40 of IFRS 16 to assess whether the lessee is reasonably certain not to exercise the option to terminate the lease. … The Committee concluded that the principles and requirements in IFRS 16 provide an adequate basis for an entity to determine the lease term of cancellable and renewable leases. The Committee also concluded that the principles and requirements in IAS 16 and IFRS 16 provide an adequate basis for an entity to determine the useful life of any non-removable leasehold improvements relating to such a lease. Consequently, the Committee decided not to add the matter to its standard-setting agenda.]\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"general_lessee_lease_model\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Modèle général du preneur\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Ce traitement s’applique dans cette situation dès lors que l’analyse de la durée de location montre que le bail renouvelable par tacite reconduction ou sans terme défini ne peut pas être qualifié de courte durée. IFRS 16 impose de rétenir la période exécutoire et les options de prolongation/résiliation raisonnablement certaines, en tenant compte des incitations économiques, notamment l’importance de l’actif, les coûts de remplacement ou les aménagements significatifs (IFRS 16 18, B37, B39, E11). Si cette analyse conduit à une durée supérieure à 12 mois, l’exemption de l’IFRS 16 5 ne peut pas être utilisée et le contrat relève du modèle général.\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"La durée de location retenue après analyse IFRS 16 dépasse 12 mois.\\\",\\n        \\\"Ou la période exécutoire du contrat excède le seul préavis en raison de pénalités économiques plus qu’insignifiantes ou d’incitations à rester.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le preneur ne peut pas utiliser l’exemption de courte durée et applique la comptabilisation générale des contrats de location.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"18\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall determine the lease term as **the non-cancellable period of a lease**, together with both: (a)periods covered by an option to extend the lease if the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise that option; and (b)periods covered by an option to terminate the lease if the lessee is reasonably certain not to exercise that option.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B37\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"At the commencement date, an entity assesses whether the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise an option to extend the lease or to purchase the underlying asset, or not to exercise an option to terminate the lease. The entity considers **all relevant facts and circumstances that create an economic incentive** for the lessee to exercise, or not to exercise, the option, including any expected changes in facts and circumstances from the commencement date until the exercise date of the option. Examples of factors to consider include, but are not limited to: (a)contractual terms and conditions for the optional periods compared with market rates, such as: (i)the amount of payments for the lease in any optional period; (ii)the amount of any variable payments for the lease or other contingent payments, such as payments resulting from termination penalties and residual value guarantees ; and (iii)the terms and conditions of any options that are exercisable after initial optional periods (for example, a purchase option that is exercisable at the end of an extension period at a rate that is currently below market rates). (b)**significant leasehold improvements** undertaken (or expected to be undertaken) over the term of the contract that are expected to have significant economic benefit for the lessee when the option to extend or terminate the lease, or to purchase the underlying asset, becomes exercisable; E11 (c)**costs relating to the termination of the lease**, such as negotiation costs, relocation costs, costs of identifying another underlying asset suitable for the lessee’s needs, costs of integrating a new asset into the lessee’s operations, or termination penalties and similar costs, including costs associated with returning the underlying asset in a contractually specified condition or to a contractually specified location; (d)**the importance of that underlying asset to the lessee’s operations**, considering, for example, whether the underlying asset is a specialised asset, the location of the underlying asset and the availability of suitable alternatives; and (e)conditionality associated with exercising the option (ie when the option can be exercised only if one or more conditions are met), and the likelihood that those conditions will exist.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B39\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The shorter the non-cancellable period of a lease, **the more likely a lessee is to exercise an option to extend the lease or not to exercise an option to terminate the lease**. This is because the costs associated with obtaining a replacement asset are likely to be proportionately higher the shorter the non-cancellable period.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"E11\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"[IFRIC® Update, November 2019, Agenda Decision, ‘ IFRS 16 Leases and IAS 16 Property, Plant and Equipment —Lease Term and Useful Life of Leasehold Improvements’ + The Committee received a request about cancellable or renewable leases. The cancellable lease described in the request is one that does not specify a particular contractual term but continues indefinitely until either party to the contract gives notice to terminate. The contract includes a notice period of, for example, less than 12 months and the contract does not oblige either party to make a payment on termination. The renewable lease described in the request is one that specifies an initial period, and renews indefinitely at the end of the initial period unless terminated by either of the parties to the contract. The request asked two questions: (a)how to determine the lease term of a cancellable lease or a renewable lease. Specifically, the request asked whether, when applying paragraph B34 of IFRS 16 and assessing ‘no more than an insignificant penalty’, an entity considers the broader economics of the contract, and not only contractual termination payments. Such considerations might include, for example, the cost of abandoning or dismantling leasehold improvements. (b)whether the useful life of any related non-removable leasehold improvements is limited to the lease term determined applying IFRS 16. Non-removable leasehold improvements are, for example, fixtures and fittings acquired by the lessee and constructed on the underlying asset that is the subject of the cancellable or renewable lease. The lessee will use and benefit from the leasehold improvements only for as long as it uses the underlying asset. ... Interaction between lease term and useful life In assessing whether a lessee is reasonably certain to extend (or not to terminate) a lease, paragraph B37 of IFRS 16 requires an entity to consider **all relevant facts and circumstances that create an economic incentive for the lessee**. This includes **significant leasehold improvements** undertaken (or expected to be undertaken) over the term of the contract that are expected to have significant economic benefit for the lessee when an option to extend or terminate the lease becomes exercisable (paragraph B37(b)). In addition, as noted above an entity considers **the broader economics of the contract** when determining the enforceable period of the lease described in the request. This includes, for example, the costs of abandoning or dismantling non-removable leasehold improvements. If an entity expects to use non-removable leasehold improvements beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated, the existence of those leasehold improvements indicates that the entity might incur a more than insignificant penalty if it terminates the lease. Consequently, applying paragraph B34 of IFRS 16, an entity considers whether the contract is enforceable for at least the period of expected utility of the leasehold improvements. In assessing whether a lessee is reasonably certain to extend (or not to terminate) a lease, paragraph B37 of IFRS 16 requires an entity to consider all relevant facts and circumstances that create an economic incentive for the lessee. This includes significant leasehold improvements undertaken (or expected to be undertaken) over the term of the contract that are expected to have significant economic benefit for the lessee when an option to extend or terminate the lease becomes exercisable (paragraph B37(b)). In addition, as noted above an entity considers the broader economics of the contract when determining the enforceable period of the lease described in the request. This includes, for example, the costs of abandoning or dismantling non-removable leasehold improvements. If an entity expects to use non-removable leasehold improvements beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated, the existence of those leasehold improvements indicates that the entity might incur a more than insignificant penalty if it terminates the lease. Consequently, applying paragraph B34 of IFRS 16, an entity considers whether the contract is enforceable for at least the period of expected utility of the leasehold improvements. The Committee concluded that the principles and requirements in IFRS 16 provide an adequate basis for an entity to determine the lease term of cancellable and renewable leases. The Committee also concluded that the principles and requirements in IAS 16 and IFRS 16 provide an adequate basis for an entity to determine the useful life of any non-removable leasehold improvements relating to such a lease. Consequently, the Committee decided not to add the matter to its standard-setting agenda.]\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778936949221}

cache: keyv:repeat:1:exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py:4606dc2dc0f5c27588a548eb91f4dfc4f2d83a6b2066151c169a0cb3228e57d0:Pour un contrat sans option d’achat renouvelable par tacite reconduction, ou conclu sans terme défini, comment apprécier si la durée de location permet l’application de l’exemption de courte durée ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_13-02-32_promptfoo-eval-family-q12"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"On suppose que le contrat ne comporte pas d’option d’achat, conformément à la question.\\\",\\n    \\\"On suppose que la question porte sur l’appréciation de la durée de location au commencement du bail, puis sur sa réévaluation si des événements ultérieurs pertinents surviennent.\\\",\\n    \\\"On suppose que le contrat est soit renouvelable par tacite reconduction, soit résiliable/renouvelable sans terme défini, de sorte que l’analyse de la période exécutoire est déterminante.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"L’exemption de courte durée ne peut être retenue que si, après détermination de la durée de location selon la période exécutoire et les options de prolongation/résiliation, le bail remplit bien les critères du bail de courte durée. Pour un bail à tacite reconduction ou sans terme défini, il ne faut pas se limiter au préavis contractuel.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"short_term_lease_exemption\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Exemption pour bail de courte durée\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette approche n’est applicable dans cette situation que si la durée de location, déterminée selon la période non résiliable et les options pertinentes, aboutit effectivement à un bail de courte durée au sens d’IFRS 16. Pour un contrat à tacite reconduction ou sans terme défini, IFRS 16 impose d’identifier d’abord la période exécutoire en appréciant si les deux parties peuvent mettre fin au contrat sans plus qu’une pénalité insignifiante, en tenant compte de l’économie globale du contrat et pas seulement des indemnités contractuelles (IFRS 16 B34, IFRS 16 E10). Ensuite, la durée de location inclut les périodes de prolongation ou de résiliation que le preneur est raisonnablement certain d’exercer ou de ne pas exercer, en tenant compte notamment des coûts de remplacement, de l’importance de l’actif et des aménagements locatifs (IFRS 16 18, IFRS 16 19, IFRS 16 B37, IFRS 16 B39).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"La durée de location déterminée selon IFRS 16 doit effectivement satisfaire aux critères du bail de courte durée.\\\",\\n        \\\"L’analyse de la période exécutoire doit montrer que le bail ne s’étend pas au-delà de la période pouvant être rompue par les deux parties sans pénalité plus qu’insignifiante.\\\",\\n        \\\"Aucun fait ni circonstance ne doit conduire à conclure que le preneur est raisonnablement certain de prolonger le bail ou de ne pas le résilier.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"L’exemption n’est possible qu’après une analyse documentée de la période exécutoire et des incitations économiques affectant la durée de location.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"5\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A lessee may elect not to apply the requirements in paragraphs 22⁠–⁠49 to: (a)**short-term leases** ; and (b)leases for which the underlying asset is of low value (as described in paragraphs B3⁠–⁠B8).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"18\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall determine the lease term as the non-cancellable period of a lease, together with both: (a)periods covered by an option to extend the lease if the lessee is **reasonably certain to exercise that option**; and (b)periods covered by an option to terminate the lease if the lessee is **reasonably certain not to exercise that option**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"19\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In assessing whether a lessee is reasonably certain to exercise an option to extend a lease, or not to exercise an option to terminate a lease, an entity shall consider **all relevant facts and circumstances that create an economic incentive** for the lessee to exercise the option to extend the lease, or not to exercise the option to terminate the lease, as described in paragraphs B37⁠–⁠B40.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B34\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In determining the lease term and assessing the length of the non-cancellable period of a lease, an entity shall apply the definition of a contract and determine the period for which the contract is **enforceable**. A lease is no longer enforceable when the lessee and the lessor each has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with **no more than an insignificant penalty**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B37\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"At the commencement date, an entity assesses whether the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise an option to extend the lease or to purchase the underlying asset, or not to exercise an option to terminate the lease. The entity considers **all relevant facts and circumstances that create an economic incentive** for the lessee to exercise, or not to exercise, the option, including any expected changes in facts and circumstances from the commencement date until the exercise date of the option. Examples of factors to consider include, but are not limited to: (a)contractual terms and conditions for the optional periods compared with market rates, such as: (i)the amount of payments for the lease in any optional period; (ii)the amount of any variable payments for the lease or other contingent payments, such as payments resulting from termination penalties and residual value guarantees ; and (iii)the terms and conditions of any options that are exercisable after initial optional periods (for example, a purchase option that is exercisable at the end of an extension period at a rate that is currently below market rates). (b)**significant leasehold improvements** undertaken (or expected to be undertaken) over the term of the contract that are expected to have significant economic benefit for the lessee when the option to extend or terminate the lease, or to purchase the underlying asset, becomes exercisable; E11 (c)**costs relating to the termination of the lease**, such as negotiation costs, relocation costs, costs of identifying another underlying asset suitable for the lessee’s needs, costs of integrating a new asset into the lessee’s operations, or termination penalties and similar costs, including costs associated with returning the underlying asset in a contractually specified condition or to a contractually specified location; (d)**the importance of that underlying asset to the lessee’s operations**, considering, for example, whether the underlying asset is a specialised asset, the location of the underlying asset and the availability of suitable alternatives; and (e)conditionality associated with exercising the option (ie when the option can be exercised only if one or more conditions are met), and the likelihood that those conditions will exist.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B39\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The **shorter the non-cancellable period** of a lease, the more likely a lessee is to exercise an option to extend the lease or not to exercise an option to terminate the lease. This is because the costs associated with obtaining a replacement asset are likely to be proportionately higher the shorter the non-cancellable period.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"E10\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The Committee observed that, in applying paragraph B34 and determining the enforceable period of the lease described in the request, an entity considers: (a)**the broader economics of the contract, and not only contractual termination payments**. For example, if either party has an economic incentive not to terminate the lease such that it would incur a penalty on termination that is more than insignificant, the contract is enforceable beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated; and (b)whether **each of the parties has the right to terminate the lease** without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty. Applying paragraph B34, a lease is no longer enforceable only when **both parties** have such a right. Consequently, if only one party has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty, the contract is enforceable beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated by that party. If an entity concludes that the contract is enforceable beyond the notice period of a cancellable lease (or the initial period of a renewable lease), it then applies paragraphs 19 and B37⁠–⁠B40 of IFRS 16 to assess whether the lessee is **reasonably certain not to exercise the option to terminate the lease**.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"general_lessee_accounting\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Comptabilisation générale du preneur\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Cette approche s’applique dans cette situation si l’analyse de la durée de location montre que le bail ne peut pas être qualifié de courte durée. C’est notamment le cas si la période exécutoire dépasse le simple préavis parce qu’une partie subirait une pénalité plus qu’insignifiante en cas de résiliation, ou si le preneur est raisonnablement certain de prolonger le bail ou de ne pas le résilier compte tenu des faits et circonstances pertinents (IFRS 16 B34, IFRS 16 E10, IFRS 16 18, IFRS 16 19, IFRS 16 B37). En outre, la durée doit être révisée en cas de changement de la période non résiliable ou d’événement significatif relevant du contrôle du preneur (IFRS 16 20, IFRS 16 21, IFRS 16 B41).\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’analyse de la durée de location conduit à écarter l’exemption de courte durée.\\\",\\n        \\\"Ou un événement ultérieur impose de réviser la durée de location et fait perdre l’éligibilité à l’exemption.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si l’exemption n’est pas justifiée après analyse de la durée, le bail relève du modèle général du preneur sous IFRS 16.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"18\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall determine the lease term as the non-cancellable period of a lease, together with both: (a)periods covered by an option to extend the lease if the lessee is **reasonably certain to exercise that option**; and (b)periods covered by an option to terminate the lease if the lessee is **reasonably certain not to exercise that option**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"19\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In assessing whether a lessee is reasonably certain to exercise an option to extend a lease, or not to exercise an option to terminate a lease, an entity shall consider **all relevant facts and circumstances that create an economic incentive** for the lessee to exercise the option to extend the lease, or not to exercise the option to terminate the lease, as described in paragraphs B37⁠–⁠B40.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"20\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A lessee shall reassess whether it is reasonably certain to exercise an extension option, or not to exercise a termination option, upon the occurrence of either a **significant event or a significant change in circumstances** that: (a)is within the control of the lessee; and (b)affects whether the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise an option not previously included in its determination of the lease term, or not to exercise an option previously included in its determination of the lease term (as described in paragraph B41).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"21\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"An entity shall **revise the lease term** if there is a change in the non-cancellable period of a lease. For example, the non-cancellable period of a lease will change if: (a)the lessee exercises an option not previously included in the entity’s determination of the lease term; (b)the lessee does not exercise an option previously included in the entity’s determination of the lease term; (c)an event occurs that contractually obliges the lessee to exercise an option not previously included in the entity’s determination of the lease term; or (d)an event occurs that contractually prohibits the lessee from exercising an option previously included in the entity’s determination of the lease term.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B34\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In determining the lease term and assessing the length of the non-cancellable period of a lease, an entity shall apply the definition of a contract and determine the period for which the contract is **enforceable**. A lease is no longer enforceable when the lessee and the lessor each has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with **no more than an insignificant penalty**.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B37\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"At the commencement date, an entity assesses whether the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise an option to extend the lease or to purchase the underlying asset, or not to exercise an option to terminate the lease. The entity considers **all relevant facts and circumstances that create an economic incentive** for the lessee to exercise, or not to exercise, the option, including any expected changes in facts and circumstances from the commencement date until the exercise date of the option. Examples of factors to consider include, but are not limited to: (a)contractual terms and conditions for the optional periods compared with market rates, such as: (i)the amount of payments for the lease in any optional period; (ii)the amount of any variable payments for the lease or other contingent payments, such as payments resulting from termination penalties and residual value guarantees ; and (iii)the terms and conditions of any options that are exercisable after initial optional periods (for example, a purchase option that is exercisable at the end of an extension period at a rate that is currently below market rates). (b)**significant leasehold improvements** undertaken (or expected to be undertaken) over the term of the contract that are expected to have significant economic benefit for the lessee when the option to extend or terminate the lease, or to purchase the underlying asset, becomes exercisable; E11 (c)**costs relating to the termination of the lease**, such as negotiation costs, relocation costs, costs of identifying another underlying asset suitable for the lessee’s needs, costs of integrating a new asset into the lessee’s operations, or termination penalties and similar costs, including costs associated with returning the underlying asset in a contractually specified condition or to a contractually specified location; (d)**the importance of that underlying asset to the lessee’s operations**, considering, for example, whether the underlying asset is a specialised asset, the location of the underlying asset and the availability of suitable alternatives; and (e)conditionality associated with exercising the option (ie when the option can be exercised only if one or more conditions are met), and the likelihood that those conditions will exist.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B41\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"Paragraph 20 specifies that, after the commencement date, a lessee reassesses the lease term upon the occurrence of a significant event or a significant change in circumstances that is within the control of the lessee and affects whether the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise an option not previously included in its determination of the lease term, or not to exercise an option previously included in its determination of the lease term. Examples of significant events or changes in circumstances include: (a)**significant leasehold improvements** not anticipated at the commencement date that are expected to have significant economic benefit for the lessee when the option to extend or terminate the lease, or to purchase the underlying asset, becomes exercisable; (b)a significant modification to, or customisation of, the underlying asset that was not anticipated at the commencement date; (c)the inception of a sublease of the underlying asset for a period beyond the end of the previously determined lease term; and (d)a business decision of the lessee that is directly relevant to exercising, or not exercising, an option (for example, a decision to extend the lease of a complementary asset, to dispose of an alternative asset or to dispose of a business unit within which the right-of-use asset is employed).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"E10\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"The Committee observed that, in applying paragraph B34 and determining the enforceable period of the lease described in the request, an entity considers: (a)**the broader economics of the contract, and not only contractual termination payments**. For example, if either party has an economic incentive not to terminate the lease such that it would incur a penalty on termination that is more than insignificant, the contract is enforceable beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated; and (b)whether **each of the parties has the right to terminate the lease** without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty. Applying paragraph B34, a lease is no longer enforceable only when **both parties** have such a right. Consequently, if only one party has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty, the contract is enforceable beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated by that party. If an entity concludes that the contract is enforceable beyond the notice period of a cancellable lease (or the initial period of a renewable lease), it then applies paragraphs 19 and B37⁠–⁠B40 of IFRS 16 to assess whether the lessee is **reasonably certain not to exercise the option to terminate the lease**.\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778937057186}

Cette caractéristique empêche-t-elle de le qualifier de contrat de courte durée au sens d’IFRS 16 ?:{"id":"exec:uv run python /Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/scripts/run_answer.py","config":{"policy-config":"./effective/policy.default.yaml","llm_provider":"openai-codex","basePath":"/Users/philippe/Documents/workspace/ifrs-expert/experiments/57_answer-evals_on_variants_for_Q5_Q6_Q9_Q12_Q16/runs/2026-05-02_13-02-32_promptfoo-eval-family-q12"},"env":{}}

cache.value: {"value":"{\"output\":\"{\\n  \\\"assumptions_fr\\\": [\\n    \\\"Le bail ne comporte pas d’option d’achat, comme indiqué dans la question.\\\",\\n    \\\"La durée initiale contractuelle est inférieure ou égale à douze mois.\\\",\\n    \\\"La poursuite automatique du bail peut résulter soit d’un mécanisme de renouvellement, soit d’un droit de résiliation avec préavis, de sorte que la durée du bail doit être appréciée au regard de la période exécutoire et des incitations économiques.\\\"\\n  ],\\n  \\\"recommendation\\\": {\\n    \\\"answer\\\": \\\"non\\\",\\n    \\\"justification\\\": \\\"La reconduction automatique n’empêche pas, à elle seule, la qualification de contrat de courte durée. Il faut déterminer la lease term en appliquant la période exécutoire et les options de terminaison/renouvellement; si cette durée n’excède pas 12 mois, l’exemption peut être retenue, sinon le modèle général s’applique.\\\"\\n  },\\n  \\\"approaches\\\": [\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_1\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"short_term_lease_exemption\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Exemption des contrats de courte durée\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"L’exemption peut s’appliquer dans cette situation seulement si, après analyse de la durée du bail, celui-ci reste d’au plus douze mois et sans option d’achat (IFRS 16 5). La reconduction automatique ne l’exclut pas mécaniquement, car la durée dépend de la période exécutoire et des droits de résiliation de chaque partie, ainsi que des incitations économiques à ne pas résilier (IFRS 16 B34, B35, B37; IFRS 16 E10). Si cette analyse conduit à une durée excédant douze mois, le bail ne peut pas être traité comme court terme.\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"La durée du bail déterminée en substance, y compris la période exécutoire et les périodes que le preneur est raisonnablement certain de conserver, ne dépasse pas douze mois.\\\",\\n        \\\"Il n’existe pas d’incitation économique plus que négligeable qui rende le contrat exécutoire au-delà de la période initiale ou du préavis de résiliation.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Si ces conditions sont remplies, les paiements de loyers peuvent être comptabilisés en charge selon IFRS 16 6.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"5\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"A lessee may elect not to apply the requirements in paragraphs 22⁠–⁠49 to: (a)**short-term leases** ; and (b)leases for which the underlying asset is of low value (as described in paragraphs B3⁠–⁠B8).\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"6\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If a lessee elects not to apply the requirements in paragraphs 22⁠–⁠49 to either short-term leases or leases for which the underlying asset is of low value, the lessee shall recognise the lease payments associated with those leases as an expense on either a straight-line basis over the lease term or another systematic basis. The lessee shall apply another systematic basis if that basis is more representative of the pattern of the lessee’s benefit.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B34\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In determining the lease term and assessing the length of the non-cancellable period of a lease, an entity shall apply the definition of a contract and determine the period for which the contract is enforceable. A lease is no longer enforceable when **the lessee and the lessor each has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty**. E10\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B35\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If only a lessee has the right to terminate a lease, that right is considered to be an option to terminate the lease available to the lessee that an entity considers when determining the lease term. **If only a lessor has the right to terminate a lease, the non-cancellable period of the lease includes the period covered by the option to terminate the lease.**\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B37\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"At the commencement date, an entity assesses whether the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise an option to extend the lease or to purchase the underlying asset, or not to exercise an option to terminate the lease. **The entity considers all relevant facts and circumstances that create an economic incentive for the lessee to exercise, or not to exercise, the option**, including any expected changes in facts and circumstances from the commencement date until the exercise date of the option.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"E10\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"[IFRIC® Update, November 2019, Agenda Decision, ‘ IFRS 16 Leases and IAS 16 Property, Plant and Equipment —Lease Term and Useful Life of Leasehold Improvements’ + The Committee observed that, in applying paragraph B34 and determining the enforceable period of the lease described in the request, an entity considers: (a)**the broader economics of the contract, and not only contractual termination payments**. For example, if either party has an economic incentive not to terminate the lease such that it would incur a penalty on termination that is more than insignificant, the contract is enforceable beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated; and (b)**whether each of the parties has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty**. Applying paragraph B34, a lease is no longer enforceable only when both parties have such a right. Consequently, if only one party has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty, the contract is enforceable beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated by that party. If an entity concludes that the contract is enforceable beyond the notice period of a cancellable lease (or the initial period of a renewable lease), it then applies paragraphs 19 and B37⁠–⁠B40 of IFRS 16 to assess whether the lessee is reasonably certain not to exercise the option to terminate the lease. …]\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    {\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"approach_2\\\",\\n      \\\"normalized_label\\\": \\\"right_of_use_asset_and_lease_liability\\\",\\n      \\\"label_fr\\\": \\\"Actif de droit d’utilisation et passif de loyer\\\",\\n      \\\"applicability\\\": \\\"oui_sous_conditions\\\",\\n      \\\"reasoning_fr\\\": \\\"Ce traitement s’applique si le bail ne peut pas bénéficier de l’exemption courte durée, c’est-à-dire si la durée du bail déterminée selon la période exécutoire et les options excède douze mois en substance (IFRS 16 B34, B37; IFRS 16 E10). IFRS 16 6 distingue expressément cette exemption du modèle général des paragraphes 22–49: en dehors de l’exemption, le preneur applique la comptabilisation normale du bail.\\\",\\n      \\\"conditions_fr\\\": [\\n        \\\"L’analyse de la période exécutoire et des incitations économiques conduit à une durée du bail supérieure à douze mois.\\\",\\n        \\\"Le preneur ne retient pas, ou ne peut pas retenir, l’exemption de courte durée.\\\"\\n      ],\\n      \\\"practical_implication_fr\\\": \\\"Le preneur comptabilise alors le bail selon le modèle général d’IFRS 16, et non en simple charge locative.\\\",\\n      \\\"references\\\": [\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"6\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"If a lessee elects not to apply the requirements in paragraphs 22⁠–⁠49 to either short-term leases or leases for which the underlying asset is of low value, the lessee shall recognise the lease payments associated with those leases as an expense on either a straight-line basis over the lease term or another systematic basis. The lessee shall apply another systematic basis if that basis is more representative of the pattern of the lessee’s benefit.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B34\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"In determining the lease term and assessing the length of the non-cancellable period of a lease, an entity shall apply the definition of a contract and determine the period for which the contract is enforceable. A lease is no longer enforceable when **the lessee and the lessor each has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty**. E10\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"B37\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"At the commencement date, an entity assesses whether the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise an option to extend the lease or to purchase the underlying asset, or not to exercise an option to terminate the lease. **The entity considers all relevant facts and circumstances that create an economic incentive for the lessee to exercise, or not to exercise, the option**, including any expected changes in facts and circumstances from the commencement date until the exercise date of the option.\\\"\\n        },\\n        {\\n          \\\"document\\\": \\\"ifrs16\\\",\\n          \\\"section\\\": \\\"E10\\\",\\n          \\\"excerpt\\\": \\\"[IFRIC® Update, November 2019, Agenda Decision, ‘ IFRS 16 Leases and IAS 16 Property, Plant and Equipment —Lease Term and Useful Life of Leasehold Improvements’ + The Committee observed that, in applying paragraph B34 and determining the enforceable period of the lease described in the request, an entity considers: (a)**the broader economics of the contract, and not only contractual termination payments**. For example, if either party has an economic incentive not to terminate the lease such that it would incur a penalty on termination that is more than insignificant, the contract is enforceable beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated; and (b)**whether each of the parties has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty**. Applying paragraph B34, a lease is no longer enforceable only when both parties have such a right. Consequently, if only one party has the right to terminate the lease without permission from the other party with no more than an insignificant penalty, the contract is enforceable beyond the date on which the contract can be terminated by that party. If an entity concludes that the contract is enforceable beyond the notice period of a cancellable lease (or the initial period of a renewable lease), it then applies paragraphs 19 and B37⁠–⁠B40 of IFRS 16 to assess whether the lessee is reasonably certain not to exercise the option to terminate the lease. …]\\\"\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  ]\\n}\"}","expires":1778937145678}
